Consider renaming to _build_parser and _read_plan, unless they are intended as public API for library consumers or tests.

title: fix: rewrite groupNodesByJob to be immutable (no .push mutation)
description: R3-HIGH-5. groupNodesByJob in layout-graph.ts:32-56 mutates objects in place: .push(node) on tasks array and reassignment of job field inside a Map. Directly violates the project's immutability-by-default principle. Fix: use functional approach with Map.groupBy: const jobs = nodes.filter(n => n.nodeKind === 'job'); const tasksByResource = Map.groupBy(tasks, t => t.resourceKey); return jobs.map(job => ({ job, tasks: tasksByResource.get(job.resourceKey) ?? [] })). Also add a comment to topologicalSortTasks acknowledging that Kahn's algorithm uses local mutable state for algorithmic efficiency (deliberate exception). Flagged by: Code Quality (HIGH).

title: Fix bun --hot HMR reload loop with elkjs
description: bun run dev triggers continuous hot reloads when elkjs is in the dependency graph. The HMR re-bundles layout-graph.ts (~2600ms each) in an infinite loop. Production build works fine. Likely a bun HMR bug with large bundled dependencies. Workaround: use bun run build + static server for now.
close_reason: Verified resolved — no HMR reload loop observed with elkjs in dependency graph. User confirmed no flickering in browser.

title: Mixed-identity embed labels pick shared attribute instead of per-entry identity
description:
## Problem

buildLabel in embed-entries.ts picks the wrong identity field for permission entries in mixed-identity __embed__ arrays.

Grants and permissions have inverted schemas:
- **Grants**: principal (identity) → privileges (value). One consistent identity field on every entry.
- **Permissions**: group_name/user_name/service_principal_name (identity, mutually exclusive) + level (value). Identity varies per entry; the shared field is the value.

findIdentityKey picks the field with the highest count across entries. For permissions, level exists on all entries with unique values → it wins over group_name (subset) and user_name (subset). Result: [level='IS_OWNER'] instead of [user_name='user1@example.com'].

When only one entry survives change-path filtering, findIdentityKey returns undefined (needs ≥2), and the last-resort fallback picks the first string field → level.

## Fixture

fixtures/golden/sub-resources now has ownership_transfer job:
- Change path: [group_name='viewers'].level (CAN_VIEW → CAN_MANAGE_RUN)
- Unchanged entries: IS_OWNER (user_name) + admins (group_name)
- IS_OWNER gets labeled [level='IS_OWNER'] — wrong

Also noop_job (action: skip, all permissions unchanged) for the display-only path.

## Where it happens

embed-entries.ts:buildLabel fallback (line ~58). Resolution order:
1. Change-path identity fields → miss (entry lacks group_name)
2. findIdentityKey → undefined (single entry) or level (multiple entries)
3. First string field → level

Needs to distinguish grants-style (uniform identity) from permissions-style (mixed identity, shared attribute).

title: Fix viewport centering offset on first node click
description: In src/components/flow-canvas.tsx:60-73, the click handler uses node.measured?.width which may not be available on first click after layout. Falls back to hardcoded 200/50 which can be wrong, causing the centering to be off. Also getZoom() may return stale value if initial fitView animation hasn't completed.

title: Use precise type annotation for _count_by_action entries param
description: text.py:140 — entries param typed as dict[str, dict] but values are plan entry dicts with an 'action' key. Bare dict is imprecise compared to other annotations in the file. Minor.
close_reason: Superseded by broader type alias effort across the codebase

title: feat: canvas background click closes detail panel
description: Clicking empty canvas space does nothing when panel is open. Standard dismissal pattern. Fix: add onPaneClick={handleClosePanel} to ReactFlow component in flow-canvas.tsx.

title: feat: support FORCE_COLOR environment variable in _supports_color()
description: R3-MED-13. _supports_color() in text.py only checks NO_COLOR and isatty(). It doesn't support FORCE_COLOR (used by CI tools and testing frameworks to enable color when stdout is not a TTY). Fix: add FORCE_COLOR check between NO_COLOR and isatty: if os.environ.get('NO_COLOR'): return False; if os.environ.get('FORCE_COLOR'): return True; return sys.stdout.isatty(). Standard convention used by chalk, pytest, GitHub Actions, etc. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Validate placeholder count in _inject_plan
description: browser.py:58 — str.replace replaces ALL occurrences of the placeholder. If __DAGSHUND_PLAN_JSON__ appears more than once in the template (e.g. a comment), JSON gets duplicated silently. Validate exactly one occurrence or use replace(..., 1).

title: fix: unhandled promise rejection in usePlanGraph/useResourceGraph
description: Both hooks call toReactFlowElements().then() with no .catch(). If ELK layout fails, the promise rejection is swallowed and the UI shows loading forever. Fix: add .catch() that sets an error state, surface it in the UI.

title: Support parent_instance_ref for Lakebase Provisioned instance chaining
description:
database_instance resources can have a parent_instance_ref.name field pointing to a parent instance (copy-on-write clone, max 1 child per parent, primarily for recovery/testing).

After dagshund-jofq restructuring, database_instances will be flat workspace resources (not hierarchy containers). The parent_instance_ref relationship becomes a non-hierarchy dependency edge between two flat resources. Could be modeled as:
- Always-visible edge (since both are in the same flat workspace bucket)
- Or part of the non-hierarchy toggle (dagshund-czfs)

Docs: https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/oltp/instances/create/child-instance
close_reason: Instances are flat compute resources — they should not be nested even if one can be based on another

title: Parse plan JSON once and pass parsed dict to renderers
description: When --detailed-exitcode is combined with text or browser output, parse_plan(raw) is called twice: once inside render_text/render_browser and again in main() at cli.py:125. Refactor so main() parses once and passes the parsed Plan dict to renderers. Flagged by guidelines, readability, and performance reviewers. Not user-noticeable at current scale but violates DRY and obscures data flow.
close_reason: Parsed once in main(), renderers accept Plan dict

title: fix: mitigate DNS rebinding on dev server
description: Dev server on 127.0.0.1:3000 has no Host header validation or CORS. DNS rebinding could let a malicious webpage read /api/plan data. Fix: validate Host header (reject anything not 127.0.0.1:3000 or localhost:3000), set Access-Control-Allow-Origin restrictively.

title: Resource graph: support all DABs resource types consistently
description:
## Problem

The resource graph hardcodes UC resource types in multiple places:
- `UC_TYPES` set (catalogs, schemas, volumes, registered_models) — anything not listed falls to workspace
- Hierarchy edge logic checks `resourceType === "catalogs"` and `resourceType === "schemas"` literally
- Phantom catalog detection filters on `extractResourceType(key) === "catalogs"` only
- Jobs are excluded entirely from the resource graph (`isJobEntry` filter)

This means new UC types like `database_catalogs` silently render in the wrong bucket, and jobs don't appear at all.

## Full resource type mapping (from Databricks docs)

Ref: https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/bundles/resources#supported-resources

### Unity Catalog hierarchy (catalog → schema → leaf)
- `catalogs` — top-level UC container
- `database_catalogs` — UC catalog backed by a Lakebase database_instance (same tier as catalogs)
- `schemas` — nest under catalogs
- `volumes`, `registered_models`, `synced_database_tables` — nest under schemas

### Workspace (flat)
- `jobs` — currently excluded from resource graph, should show as single nodes
- `pipelines`, `clusters`, `apps`, `dashboards`, `alerts`, `experiments`
- `model_serving_endpoints`, `sql_warehouses`, `secret_scopes`
- `database_instances` — Lakebase compute/storage, workspace-level
- `postgres_projects`, `postgres_endpoints`, `postgres_branches` — workspace-level
- `quality_monitors` — references UC tables but lives at workspace level

### Cross-graph edges
- `database_catalogs` references `database_instance_name` (workspace) — potential cross-graph edge

## What needs to change

1. **UC_TYPES**: add `database_catalogs` and `synced_database_tables`
2. **Catalog-tier detection**: treat both `catalogs` and `database_catalogs` as catalog-tier nodes (group nodes, phantom detection, hierarchy edges)
3. **Schema-tier detection**: already works for `schemas`, no change needed
4. **Leaf-tier detection**: `synced_database_tables` should behave like volumes (nest under schema)
5. **Jobs in resource graph**: remove the `isJobEntry` filter, render jobs as single nodes in the workspace bucket
6. **Make it consistent**: the categorization and hierarchy logic should be driven by data (sets/maps), not scattered string literals

## Scope
All changes in `src/graph/build-resource-graph.ts` and tests. The rendering components already handle external/phantom nodes generically.

title: Improve node alignment in fan-out/fan-in DAG patterns
description: Nodes in fan-out/fan-in patterns (e.g. data_quality_pipeline) are not perfectly centered on their layer grid. Single nodes like aggregate_results and generate_report can appear off-center relative to their incoming edges. Current ELK config uses BRANDES_KOEPF BALANCED with model-order options which is decent but not perfect. Investigate further ELK tuning or post-layout adjustment to improve vertical centering of nodes relative to their connections.
close_reason: Current ELK layout is sufficient; no clear improvement path

title: visual: thicken border on hovered node for clearer hover feedback
description: When hovering a node, the border should become slightly thicker (e.g. border-3 or outline) to give a clear visual signal of what is being hovered. Applies to job containers, task nodes, resource nodes, and resource group nodes. Currently hover only shows shadow-md which can be subtle.
notes: Implemented hover ring (1.5px box-shadow in diff-state color, debounced 50ms via React state), selection ring (2.5px when detail panel open), and selection dimming (non-connected nodes at 50% opacity, edges at 30%). Hover overrides selection dimming. Added hoverGlow to DiffStateStyles, selectedNodeId/selectedConnectedIds to HoverContext, isHovered/isSelected/dimOpacity to useNodeDimming. Commit: 5a88743

title: fix: make structural diff wrapping responsive to panel width instead of hardcoded 40 chars
description: R3-MED-22. MAX_LINE_CHARS = 40 in components/structural-diff-view.tsx:19 is very aggressive. Comment says 'Conservative for the 380px detail panel' but the panel defaults to 390px and can be resized up to 600px. Content wraps at 40 chars even when the panel is wide. Fix: either increase to 60 chars (fits in 300px min-width at ~7.2px/char mono), or make it responsive by reading the container width via a ref and computing chars that fit. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Clean up re-exports in build-resource-graph.ts
description: build-resource-graph.ts re-exports extractResourceState, extractSourceTableFullName, extractStateField, parseThreePartName from extract-resource-state.ts for backward compatibility. Once all consumers import directly from extract-resource-state.ts, remove the re-exports.

title: refactor: use collections.Counter in _count_by_action
description: R3-MED-23. _count_by_action in text.py:132-140 manually builds a count dict with get/set. More Pythonic and immutable: use collections.Counter. Fix: return dict(Counter(entry.get('action') or 'unchanged' for entry in entries.values())). 3-line fix. Flagged by: Code Quality (HIGH).

title: Nodes collapse to (0,0) when hovering after zoom-out
description:
When zoomed out fully and hovering over a node, all nodes collapse to position (0,0) in the top-left corner of the viewport. This was also seen earlier when the requestAnimationFrame ResizeObserver patch was active, but it's now happening without it.

Likely cause: the hover styling logic in DagView applies opacity changes via styledNodes/styledEdges useMemo. Something in that code path or in React Flow's internal re-measure cycle may be resetting node positions when the viewport scale is small enough.

title: job→job lateral spec for run_job_task
description:
Jobs that trigger other jobs via run_job_task currently get a task→job DAG edge in plan_graph but NO lateral edge in resource_graph. When viewing resources at the job-card level, orchestrated jobs appear disconnected.

Discovered while reviewing the all-create fixture (vi8p). The fixture was extended with etl_pipeline → run_job_task → data_quality_pipeline so this case would be exercised in tests going forward; once a lateral spec exists, the regenerated expected-graph.json will include the edge.

Design constraint from user: be DRY. We just refactored the lateral + phantom resolvers; reuse them. Do not duplicate vars-interpolation logic or create a parallel resolver.

Plan in /home/chinchy/.claude/plans/graceful-toasting-journal.md. Summary:
- Relocate resolveRunJobTarget + buildJobIdMap + parseResourceReference + resolveRunJobTargetFromVars from build-plan-graph.ts into new js/src/graph/resolve-run-job-target.ts
- Extend createJobTaskRefsSpec to handle run_job_task.job_id as a 4th branch (same dedup pattern)
- Extend collectPhantomExternalRefs to emit job::<id> phantoms for unresolvable external numeric job IDs
- Thread jobIdMap through build-resource-graph.ts alongside existing warehouseIndex/dashboardIndex/pipelineIndex
- Tests extend the existing lateral + phantom test files

Plan-graph / job-page behavior is unchanged — the four functions stay behavior-identical, only their home module changes. Existing build-plan-graph.test.ts acts as the regression check.

title: Add model_serving_endpoint → registered_model edge via entity_name
description: Model serving endpoints reference registered models via config.served_entities[].entity_name. This model→serving relationship is not currently modeled in the resource graph.
close_reason: Consolidated into dagshund-czfs (toggle for non-hierarchy relationships)

title: Move stripTaskPrefix from change-entry.tsx to utils/task-key.ts
Collapse large collections in transition diffs (list[object] readability)
description:
## Status (2026-04-14)

Shipped the minimal collapse-symmetry fix instead of the full element-wise diff (options A/B/C below).

### What changed

`format_display_value` now collapses dict/list values whose inline form exceeds `_INLINE_LIMIT` (60 chars) to `{N fields}` / `[N items]`, matching the behavior that `format_single_value` has always had for add-only/delete-only cases. `format_transition` routes through `format_display_value`, so transition diffs inherit the collapse for free.

Result for app-dependencies/expected.txt:

```
~ resources: [3 items] -> [6 items]
```

Instead of the previous two-line full-array dump.

### Why not full element-wise diff

Terminals can't show full structural diffs — it's just too much data for the CLI. The project convention everywhere else is "inline when small, summary when large". Doing element-wise `+ item / - item / ~ item` for list[object] diffs would be inconsistent with how every other collapsed value is rendered, and would drop huge walls of text into the terminal on large resource updates. The browser/DAG view is the right place for element-level structure — each app→resource ref is already an independent lateral edge there.

### Asymmetric collapse (kept)

Sides of a transition are collapsed independently:
- `[{task_key: "check_nulls"}] -> [3 items]` — left stays inline (short), right collapses (long)

This is an advantage: the shorter side often shows enough context to infer what changed (e.g. "had one task, now has three of something").

### Affected golden fixtures (regenerated)

- app-dependencies (resources array)
- task-dag-rewiring (depends_on)
- mixed-changes (depends_on)

### Out of scope (not implemented — see "why not" above)

- Element-wise `+ / - / ~` per list element
- Identity-key inference (name / task_key / key / id)
- Set-diff for lists of scalars
- Fallback paths for missing or duplicate identity keys

title: Show task-level changes summary on job group nodes
description:
When clicking a job group node (e.g. resources.jobs.data_quality_pipeline) that shows as 'modified', the detail panel currently only shows job-level changes (like depends_on updates) and unchanged job config fields. It does NOT show what changed inside the job at the task level.

For a modified job, the user wants to see a summary of task-level changes directly on the job node's detail panel:
- Added tasks: e.g. "+ check_duplicates, + check_references, + anomaly_detection" (green)
- Removed tasks: e.g. "- old_validation_task" (red)
- Modified tasks: e.g. "~ compute_statistics (depends_on changed)" (amber)

This gives a birds-eye view of what happened inside the job without clicking individual task nodes.

Similarly for added jobs: show all the tasks that were created as part of the job.
For removed jobs: show all the tasks that were deleted.

Implementation considerations:
- Job group nodes already have access to all their child task nodes in the graph
- Need to collect task-level diff states from child nodes and summarize them
- Could be a new section in the detail panel between job-level changes and unchanged config
- The task names should probably be clickable to navigate to that task node

Key files:
- src/components/detail-panel.tsx (add task summary section)
- src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts (job nodes may need references to child task diff states)
- src/types/graph-types.ts (DagNodeData may need child task summary info)

title: Derive phantom branch project from resource path
description:
Phantom branches (branches referenced by endpoints but not in the plan) currently attach directly to the Lakebase root because deriveParentRef is undefined — the branch identity alone (e.g. 'archive') doesn't encode which project it belongs to.

Now that parent fields use resource paths (e.g. projects/warehouse-replica/branches/archive), we have enough information to derive the project. The endpoint's parent path contains both project_id and branch_id.

Approach: make branch identities composite ({project_id}/{branch_id}) so deriveParentRef can split on '/' to extract the project_id. This requires:
- Branch resolveIdentity: extract project from parent field + branch_id → 'warehouse-replica/archive'
- Endpoint resolveParentRef: return composite identity matching branch format
- Branch deriveParentRef: identity.split('/')[0] → project_id
- Update phantom label extraction (currently splits on '.', needs to handle '/' separator)
- Update buildHierarchyId for composite identity
close_reason: Composite branch identities implemented, phantom branches now attach to their project

title: Replace vendored dagre.js with proper dependency
description:
The vendored src/vendor/dagre.js (2200 lines of CJS-to-ESM shim) is a hack to work around @dagrejs/dagre v2's ESM dist using dynamic require() that Bun's browser bundler can't trace. Investigate and test alternatives in order of least invasive:

1. **Direct import test** — try importing @dagrejs/dagre directly (no vendor) to see if Bun 1.3.8 handles it now
2. **esbuild prebundle as build step** — `bunx esbuild node_modules/@dagrejs/dagre/dist/dagre.cjs.js --bundle --format=esm --platform=browser --outfile=src/vendor/dagre.js` as a reproducible build:vendor script instead of a committed 2200-line file
3. **esm.sh CDN import** — `import dagre from 'https://esm.sh/@dagrejs/dagre'` to bypass bundler entirely (downside: requires internet)
4. **Switch to elkjs** — proper ESM, recommended by React Flow, more capable. Async API (returns Promise). ~30 line migration.

Test each option against `bun build` and `bun run dev`. Pick the least invasive one that works. If none of 1-3 work cleanly, go with elkjs.
close_reason: Superseded: switching to elkjs instead of fixing dagre vendor shim

title: fix: add loading indicator to FlowCanvas during ELK layout computation
description: R3-HIGH-6. After the plan loads, FlowCanvas shows an empty canvas with zero-count filter badges while ELK computes layout. GraphLayoutState starts as {status:'loading'} but FlowCanvas only handles the 'error' case — when loading, nodes/edges default to EMPTY_NODES/EMPTY_EDGES so React Flow renders blank. Fix: when layoutState.status === 'loading', render a centered 'Computing layout...' message with a pulse animation instead of the empty ReactFlow canvas. The existing LoadingIndicator in App.tsx only covers the stdin fetch phase, not the layout phase. Flagged by: UX (HIGH).

title: fix: remove .yaml/.yml from ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS in cli.ts or add clear error
description: R3-LOW-26. ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS in cli.ts:33 includes .yaml and .yml but parsePlanFromString only handles JSON via JSON.parse. A YAML file would fail with 'Invalid JSON' rather than 'YAML not supported'. Fix: either remove .yaml/.yml from the set, or detect YAML extensions and print a clear error: 'dagshund: YAML input not supported, convert to JSON first'. Flagged by: Code Quality (LOW).

title: Phantom catalog nodes in resource graph
fix: show empty state message when a tab has no nodes
description: R3-MED-15. When a plan has no jobs, the Jobs tab shows an empty React Flow canvas with zero-count filter badges — looks broken. Fix: in FlowCanvas, when baseNodes.length === 0 and layoutState.status === 'ready', render a centered message like 'No jobs in this plan' or 'No resources in this plan' instead of the empty canvas. Need to pass a contextual label (the tab name) to FlowCanvas so the message is specific. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Add a complex multi-job fixture for testing
notes:
Current test fixture is a single job with a handful of tasks. A richer fixture would help stress-test layout, hover dimming, and the detail panel.

## Structure

**Job 1: ETL_PIPELINE (existing job, mostly unchanged)**
- Keep current tasks as-is
- Add a new task that triggers Job 2 via run_job_task referencing ${resources.jobs.DATA_QUALITY_PIPELINE} (DAB cross-job linking syntax)
- This trigger task should be 'added' diff state (new resource)

**Job 2: DATA_QUALITY_PIPELINE (new complex job)**
- 8-10 tasks with realistic Databricks names (validate_schema, check_nulls, compute_statistics, etc.)
- Deep dependency chain (4+ levels) to test ELK layout spacing
- Fan-in pattern: multiple validation tasks feed into a single aggregation task
- Fan-out pattern: one setup task spawns several parallel checks
- Mix of diff states: some added, some modified, one removed, rest unchanged
- Tasks with large change payloads (long field lists) to exercise detail panel scrolling
- Realistic notebook_task / python_wheel_task / run_job_task types

## Goal
Test cross-job edges, complex layout, and all visual states in a single fixture.

title: feat: keyboard navigation for graph nodes
description: All node components are divs with no role, tabIndex, or keyboard handlers. Users cannot open the detail panel without a mouse. Fix: add role='button' tabIndex={0} to node root divs. Wire up onKeyDown (Enter/Space) to open detail panel. Leverage React Flow's nodesFocusable prop.

title: fix: use pytest.skip() instead of silent return in test_browser_mode_file_output
description: R3-MED-18. test_browser_mode_file_output in tests/test_cli.py does 'if not template.exists(): return' which silently passes the test if template.html hasn't been built. CI could show green with zero browser-mode coverage. Fix: replace with pytest.skip('template.html not built; run just template first'). This makes test runners clearly report the skip. Located at tests/test_cli.py around line 72. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: Golden fixtures: fill coverage gaps for lateral edges, phantoms, and cross-job vars
description:
All fixtures generated from scratch using the regeneration tooling (dagshund-2z0m). Existing fixtures like all-hierarchies-plan.json serve as reference for the kind of coverage we want, but nothing carries forward — everything is regenerated from real bundle configs via deploy/plan/capture/destroy.

## Golden output generation (owned by this bead)

After each plan.json is captured, run dagshund against it to produce expected terminal and markdown output:
- `dagshund < plan.json > expected.txt`
- `dagshund --format markdown < plan.json > expected.md`

These are committed alongside the plan.json in each fixture folder. CI tests (dagshund-39ue) diff against these files.

## Cleanup (owned by this bead)

When the golden suite is complete, delete all old fixtures and golden outputs:
- `fixtures/*.json` (old fixture plans)
- `fixtures/bases/` (old bundle configs)
- `tests/golden/*.txt` and `tests/golden/*.md` (old expected outputs)

The golden suite in `fixtures/golden/<name>/` replaces everything.

## Tier 1: Free Edition (generate locally)

### Diff state coverage
1. **all-create** — Fresh deploy, everything new. Jobs, schemas, volumes, experiments, registered_models.
2. **all-destroy** — Tear down everything. Same resources, all deleted.
3. **mixed-changes** — Adds + removes + updates + unchanged in one plan. Jobs with task changes, schema property edits, volume add/remove.
4. **no-changes** — Plan with zero diff. Everything skip/no_change.
5. **drift** — Remote state diverged from local config. Jobs or schemas modified out-of-band.

### Task dependency patterns
6. **task-dag-rewiring** — Tasks added/removed mid-DAG, edges change shape between before/after. Covers: linear chain, fan-out (1→N), fan-in (N→1), diamond (fan-out then fan-in).
7. **cross-job-interpolation** — run_job_task with ${resources.jobs.X.id} — job invoking another job.
8. **cross-job-new-target** — run_job_task where target job is newly created (id=0, vars-based resolution).

### Sub-resources
9. **permissions-lifecycle** — Permissions added, changed, removed on a parent that itself may or may not change.
10. **grants-lifecycle** — Grants added/changed/removed on schemas.

### Lateral dependencies (dagshund inventions, free-tier resources)
11. **alert-to-warehouse** — alert → sql_warehouse via warehouse_id. Phantom sql_warehouse if warehouse not in plan.
12. **app-dependencies** — app → jobs, warehouses, secret_scopes, endpoints, experiments. Phantom job, phantom secret_scope, phantom experiment, phantom warehouse, phantom endpoint.

### Hierarchy phantoms
13. **phantom-catalog-chain** — Volume/schema references catalog not in plan → phantom catalog. Tests UC hierarchy phantom generation.

## Tier 2: Enterprise Only (requires access to enterprise)

14. **pipeline-to-catalog** — pipeline → catalog/schema lateral edge, phantom catalog/schema. Requires Lakeflow pipelines.
15. **serving-endpoint-to-model** — model_serving_endpoint → registered_model lateral edge, phantom endpoint. Requires GPU/provisioned endpoints.
16. **lakebase-hierarchy** — synced_database_table → database_instance, source_table phantoms, postgres project/branch/endpoint hierarchy. Lakebase is enterprise-only. Reference: current all-hierarchies-plan.json shows this kind of coverage.
17. **external-locations** — External locations, advanced UC features. Enterprise UC features.

## Coverage crosscheck

| Dagshund concept | Fixture(s) |
|---|---|
| Diff states: create, delete, update, skip, drift | 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 |
| Task deps: linear, fan-out, fan-in, diamond | 6 |
| Cross-job: interpolation, new target | 7, 8 |
| Sub-resources: permissions, grants | 9, 10 |
| Lateral: alert→warehouse | 11 |
| Lateral: app→everything | 12 |
| Lateral: pipeline→catalog/schema | 14 (enterprise) |
| Lateral: endpoint→model | 15 (enterprise) |
| Lateral: synced_table→db_instance | 16 (enterprise) |
| Lateral: synced_table→source_table | 16 (enterprise) |
| Phantom: catalog, schema | 13 |
| Phantom: job, warehouse, secret_scope, experiment, endpoint | 11, 12 |
| Phantom: source_table, database_instance | 16 (enterprise) |
| Phantom: postgres project/branch | 16 (enterprise) |

Free edition limitations: https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/getting-started/free-edition-limitations
Free tier supports jobs (5 concurrent tasks), schemas, volumes, registered_models, experiments, alerts, apps (1), pipelines (1 active), model_serving_endpoints (limited, no GPU), sql_warehouses (1, 2X-Small).
Enterprise-only: Lakebase (database_instances, synced_database_tables, postgres hierarchy), external_locations, advanced UC.

17 total fixtures: 13 free-tier + 4 enterprise.

title: fix: detail panel scroll position resets on node switch
description: When clicking Node A then Node B, the detail panel doesn't scroll to top. User sees middle of Node B's content. Fix: add key={selectedNode.resourceKey} to DetailPanel to remount on change, or useEffect to scroll to top when data changes.

title: Detail panel: show full resource state, not just changes
description:
Currently the detail panel only shows diff changes — clicking an unchanged node like 'load' shows nothing useful. The panel should display:

1. **Current state** — all fields/config of the resource as they exist (baseline)
2. **Updates** — fields that changed, showing before → after
3. **Additions** — new fields that were added

This gives full visibility into what a resource looks like, not just what changed. For unchanged nodes the panel should still show the full resource configuration. For modified nodes it should highlight which fields changed within the full context.

title: Normalize phantom ID prefixes to consistent {type}:: pattern
description: Replaced inconsistent phantom ID prefixes: external:: → schema:: for UC schemas, external::postgres-branch:: → postgres-branch:: for postgres branches. All phantom types now use {type}:: convention (catalog::, schema::, source-table::, postgres-project::, postgres-branch::). Badge resolver no longer needs ordering tricks.

title: Fix diff filter toolbar clipping into job nodes on scroll
description: The Added/Modified/Removed filter buttons are rendered inside a React Flow Panel, which is positioned inside the canvas. title: Add toggle to show non-hierarchy resource relationships
description:
Add a UI toggle button that reveals non-hierarchy edges between resources. These are compute bindings, resource references, and infrastructure dependencies that don't affect the tree structure but show how resources are connected.

**Compute/infrastructure dependencies:**
- alert → sql_warehouse (via warehouse_id)
- dashboard → sql_warehouse (via warehouse_id)
- dashboard → catalog/schema (via dataset_catalog, dataset_schema)
- synced_database_table → database_instance (via database_instance_name) — instance is storage+compute, not hierarchy
- database_catalog → database_instance (via database_instance_name) — same: infrastructure dependency

**Resource bindings:**
- model_serving_endpoint → registered_model (via config.served_entities[].entity_name)
- app → job (via resources[].job.id)
- app → sql_warehouse (via resources[].sql_warehouse.id)
- app → serving_endpoint (via resources[].serving_endpoint.name)
- app → database_instance (via resources[].database.instance_name)
- app → experiment (via resources[].experiment.experiment_id)

**Write targets:**
- pipeline → catalog/schema (via catalog, schema, target, event_log.catalog, event_log.schema, ingestion_definition.objects[].destination_catalog/schema)
- job → write targets (similar to pipeline)

These edges should only appear when the toggle is active. Note: database_instance_name edges were previously modeled as hierarchy edges in the Lakebase Provisioned tree — they should become toggle-only non-hierarchy edges after dagshund-jofq restructuring.

title: Build graph model: Plan to React Flow nodes and edges
description:
Transform a parsed Plan (from parser/) into React Flow-compatible nodes and edges. All pure functions, no React code.

WHAT EXISTS:
- Plan type with record of PlanEntry (keyed by resource path string like 'resources.jobs.etl_pipeline')
- Each PlanEntry has: id, depends_on, action, changes, new_state, remote_state
- DiffState type: added | removed | modified | unchanged
- mapActionToDiffState function in parser/map-diff-state.ts
- Two real fixtures: sample-plan.json (all creates) and mixed-plan.json (updates/skips/removals)

KEY INSIGHT — THE DATA HAS THREE LEVELS:
1. Resource level: top-level plan entries keyed like 'resources.jobs.etl_pipeline'. Each has an action (create/update/delete/skip).
2. Task level: inside new_state.value.tasks, each task has task_key, depends_on (array of {task_key}), notebook_task, etc. These form the actual DAG the user wants to see.
3. Change level: the 'changes' record has keys like 'tasks[task_key=extract].notebook_task.notebook_path' with per-field actions (update/skip/delete). This tells us WHICH tasks and fields changed.

THE GRAPH MODEL MUST:
- Extract tasks from new_state.value.tasks as individual graph nodes
- Build edges from each task's depends_on array (task_key references)
- Determine each task's DiffState by checking the changes record:
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X]' with action=update and only a 'new' field (no 'old') → added
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X]' with action=update and only an 'old' field (no 'new') → removed
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X].some.field' with action=update → modified
  - If all changes for task X are action=skip → unchanged
  - If the resource-level action is 'create' → all tasks are added
- Also produce a job-level metadata node showing the resource itself (name, triggers, etc.) — this is context, not part of the task DAG edges
- Handle multiple jobs in the plan (each is a separate sub-graph)

WHAT TO BUILD in src/graph/:
1. Graph types in src/types/graph-types.ts:
   - GraphNode: id, label, nodeKind ('job' | 'task'), diffState, resourceKey, taskKey?, raw data ref
   - GraphEdge: id, source, target, label?
   - PlanGraph: { readonly nodes: readonly GraphNode[], readonly edges: readonly GraphEdge[] }
2. extractTaskNodes(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → readonly GraphNode[]
   - Dig into new_state.value.tasks, create a node per task
   - Compute diffState per task from the changes record
3. extractTaskEdges(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → readonly GraphEdge[]
   - For each task with depends_on, create source→target edges using task_key
4. extractJobNode(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → GraphNode
   - A single node representing the job itself with its resource-level diffState
5. buildPlanGraph(plan: Plan) → PlanGraph
   - Iterate all plan entries, call the above for each, combine into one graph
6. toReactFlowElements(graph: PlanGraph) → { nodes: Node[], edges: Edge[] }
   - Convert to React Flow format with dagre-computed positions
   - Must handle hundreds of nodes

FIXTURES TO TEST AGAINST:
- tests/fixtures/sample-plan.json — single job, action=create, 4 tasks (extract→transform→load→validate)
- tests/fixtures/mixed-plan.json — single job, action=update, changes include: transform notebook_path updated, aggregate task added (new only), validate task removed (old only), extract/load unchanged (all skips)

CONSTRAINTS:
- All readonly types, all pure functions, no classes
- Functions under 20 lines, verb-first camelCase naming
- Use Result type for anything that can fail
- No any, no type assertions without comment
- Dagre is a new dependency — @dagrejs/dagre
- new_state.value is typed as unknown in the schema — will need careful narrowing (Zod parse or type guard) to extract tasks safely
close_reason: Graph model complete: types, task extraction, diff state resolution, plan graph builder, dagre layout. 21 new tests, all 46 passing, tsc clean.

title: Click task → slide-in detail panel showing changes
description:
When a user clicks a task node, a detail panel slides in from the right side showing all changes for that task.

### Behavior
- Click a task node → panel slides in from the right (~350-400px wide)
- Panel shows: task name, diff state badge, and a list of all changes
- Each change entry shows: field path, old value, new value, action type
- Click outside panel or click an X button → panel closes
- Clicking a different task updates the panel contents
- Clicking a job container node could show job-level changes

### Data
The `changes` field is already computed and attached to each node's data (`DagNodeData.changes`). It's a `Record<string, ChangeDesc>` where ChangeDesc has old/new values and action.

### Files to create/modify
- `src/components/detail-panel.tsx` — new component for the slide-in panel
- `src/App.tsx` — add state for selected node, render panel conditionally
- `src/hooks/use-selected-node.ts` — optional hook to manage selection state
- `src/components/index.ts` — barrel export

### Styling
Dark theme consistent with existing zinc-950 background. Panel should have a subtle border-left separator. Changes displayed in a clean diff-like format.
close_reason: Detail panel implemented and verified in browser

title: Python package wrapper for PyPI/uvx distribution
description:
Create a Python package that bundles pre-built JS/CSS assets and provides a CLI entry point, enabling installation via uvx dagshund or pip install dagshund. No Node/Bun runtime needed on the user's machine.

Target UX:
  databricks bundle plan -o json | uvx dagshund
  uvx dagshund plan.json
  uvx dagshund plan.json -o report.html

Architecture:
- Python package with zero dependencies (just string templating + webbrowser)
- Pre-built HTML template with placeholder for plan JSON injection
- CLI reads plan from stdin or file arg, injects into template, opens browser
- Package structure: pyproject.toml + src/dagshund/{__init__.py, cli.py, _assets/template.html}
- Build pipeline: bun run build -> assemble template HTML with placeholder -> commit into _assets/
- ~2.5MB package size (bundled JS)

Requires further evaluation:
- Template generation script (similar to cli.ts but outputs placeholder instead of real plan)
- PyPI publishing workflow (CI/CD for build + publish)
- Whether to keep the dev server mode (Python HTTP server) or export-only
- Version sync between TS and Python package
- Whether to add optional plan validation in Python (via json schema) or rely on JS-side Zod
notes: Requires further evaluation before implementation. See conversation for full design discussion.

title: Fix task extraction failing on numeric job_id in real plan data
Visualize added/removed edges on modified tasks
description:
Currently edge diff state is inferred from endpoint nodes only (resolveEdgeDiffState in diff-state-styles.ts). If either endpoint is added/removed, the edge gets that state. But when a MODIFIED task changes its depends_on array (adds or removes a dependency), the edge has no diff state because both endpoint nodes still exist.

Example: task A depends_on [B, C]. After modification, task A depends_on [B, D]. The edge A→C should show as red/removed (ghost edge), and A→D should show as green/added. Currently A→C just disappears and A→D appears as gray/unchanged.

Implementation requires:
1. Compare old depends_on (from change old_value) vs new depends_on (from new_value) for modified tasks
2. Create ghost edges for removed dependencies — these edges exist in old state but not new state. Render red with reduced opacity.
3. Mark newly added edges (exist in new state but not old state) as added. Render green.
4. GraphEdge type needs a diffState field: 'added' | 'removed' | 'unchanged'
5. toFlowEdges() in layout-graph.ts needs to use the edge's own diffState when present, falling back to the current endpoint-inference logic

Key files:
- src/types/graph-types.ts (add diffState to GraphEdge)
- src/graph/extract-tasks.ts (extract old/new depends_on from changes)
- src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts (compare old vs new depends_on, create ghost edges, mark new edges)
- src/graph/layout-graph.ts (toFlowEdges should respect edge-level diffState)
- src/components/diff-state-styles.ts (resolveEdgeDiffState already handles the styling, just needs to check edge's own diffState first)
close_reason: Merged into dagshund-o4z — implementing ghost nodes and ghost edges together

title: Replace Any type aliases with proper typed structures
description:
## Problem

The foundational type aliases in `src/dagshund/__init__.py` use `Any`, violating the project's "unknown over any" guideline:

```python
from typing import Any

type ResourceChange = dict[str, Any]
type ResourceChangeMap = dict[str, ResourceChange]
type Plan = dict[str, Any]
```

This means every downstream access (`data.get("plan", {})`, `entry.get("action", "")`, etc.) is completely untyped. The TypeScript side has Zod schema validation at boundaries; the Python side has nothing equivalent.

## What to Do

Replace `Any`-based type aliases with properly typed structures. Options:

### Option A: TypedDict (recommended)
```python
from typing import TypedDict, NotRequired

class FieldChange(TypedDict):
    action: NotRequired[str]
    old: NotRequired[object]
    new: NotRequired[object]

class ResourceChange(TypedDict):
    action: NotRequired[str]
    changes: NotRequired[dict[str, FieldChange]]

type ResourceChangeMap = dict[str, ResourceChange]

class Plan(TypedDict):
    plan: NotRequired[ResourceChangeMap]
    cli_version: NotRequired[str]
    # ... other known top-level keys
```

Note: TypedDict uses `class` syntax but is NOT OOP — it's purely a type-level construct with no runtime behavior, no methods, no inheritance hierarchy. It's the Python equivalent of a TypeScript `interface` or a Zod schema. This is consistent with the project philosophy (same as how the TS side uses `interface` and `type` without it being OOP).

### Option B: Use `object` instead of `Any`
Minimal change: replace `Any` with `object` everywhere, forcing runtime type narrowing (`isinstance` checks) before use. Less ergonomic but maximally strict.

## Files That Will Need Updates
- `src/dagshund/__init__.py` — type definitions + `parse_plan` return type
- `src/dagshund/text.py` — all functions consuming ResourceChange/Plan
- `src/dagshund/browser.py` — `_inject_plan` parameter type
- `src/dagshund/cli.py` — if Plan type changes

## Acceptance Criteria
- No `Any` import or usage in `src/dagshund/`
- All type annotations use the new typed structures
- `just check` passes (including `ty` type checker)
- Existing tests pass without modification
close_reason: Won't fix: the plan data is genuinely Any from our perspective — we don't control the Databricks CLI schema. Value fields (old/new/remote) are polymorphic (strings, ints, dicts, lists). TypedDict would still need Any for those, giving minimal benefit for the complexity. Runtime isinstance guards from the input hardening issue handle malformed data.

title: Design CI/CD pipeline structure
description:
Final pipeline design for watertight CI. Output of this bead is the design below; implementation lands via dagshund-39ue (golden + CI refactor) and dagshund-21qe (browser smoke). This bead stays open until the human approves closing.

## Pipeline structure

| Trigger | Jobs | Target duration |
|---|---|---|
| Feature branch push (non-docs/beads) | quality + golden | ~45s |
| PR opened / PR push | quality + golden + security + browser | ~3 min |
| Merge to main (non-docs/beads) | pages | ~45s |
| Tag v* | version-check + build + smoke-wheel + publish-test + publish (unchanged) | ~2 min + manual gates |

## Workflow rules (mandatory)

- **Docs/beads skip:** pipeline does not run when every changed file matches \`**.md\` or \`.beads/**\`. Any non-matching file triggers normal flow. Applies to feature branches, MRs, AND main pushes (so chore(beads): sync does not rebuild pages).
- **MR-then-branch:** if an MR is open for the branch, the branch pipeline does not run — only the MR pipeline runs. Prevents doubled cost on PR pushes.
- **Interruptible:** all non-release jobs (interruptible: true). Release jobs (build, smoke-wheel, publish-test, publish) are NOT interruptible.

## Job definitions

### quality (feature branch + MR)
Lint + typecheck + unit tests as one job:
- biome check + ruff check
- tsc --noEmit + ty check (typecheck is mandatory — prek can be bypassed)
- bun test + pytest

### golden (feature branch + MR) — renamed from smoke-text
- text/md diffing (exists today)
- NEW: exit code assertions (dagshund -e vs expected-exit.txt)
- NEW: graph structure assertions (Bun extractor vs expected-graph.json)
- NEW: filter variant diffing (fixtures/golden/filters/ only)

### security (MR only — moved from every push)
sast + secret_detection. Runs scan against full repo state, not diff — redundant on feature branches when MR pipeline runs on the same commit.

### browser (MR only) — new
build-template + Playwright headless Chromium over 3 fixtures (mixed-changes, all-hierarchies, no-changes). Asserts no console errors + node count > 0 + diff badges present. Dedicated Playwright image, version-pinned (never :latest).

### pages (main only, now subject to docs/beads skip)

### Release chain (tag only, unchanged)
version-check → build → smoke-wheel → publish-test → publish.

## Golden fixture contract

Each fixtures/golden/<name>/ contains:
- before/databricks.yaml
- after/databricks.yaml
- plan.json
- expected.txt
- expected.md
- expected-exit.txt (single line: 0, 2, or 3)
- expected-graph.json (node/edge/lateral/phantom counts + manifest)

### Filter variants — one dedicated fixture only
fixtures/golden/filters/ additionally contains:
- expected.changes-only.txt
- expected.added.txt
- expected.removed.txt
- expected.modified.txt
- expected.filter-type-jobs.txt
- expected.filter-field.txt

Dot-separated naming. Other fixtures do NOT have filter variants.

### expected-graph.json shape
{
  \"nodes\": { \"total\": N, \"by_kind\": { \"job\": N, \"task\": N, \"alert\": N, \"phantom\": N } },
  \"edges\": { \"total\": N, \"dag\": N, \"lateral\": N },
  \"laterals\": [ { \"source\": \"...\", \"target\": \"...\", \"kind\": \"...\" } ],
  \"phantoms\": [ { \"id\": \"...\", \"kind\": \"...\" } ]
}

Extractor is a Bun script that imports the same graph-builder modules the browser uses, prints JSON to stdout.

## Caching (mandatory, budget-critical)

.release-setup before_script currently re-downloads bun and runs fresh uv sync + bun install every job. Add cache: directives keyed on uv.lock + js/bun.lock, caching .uv-cache/ + js/node_modules/. Set UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache in variables. Saves ~30-60s per job extending .release-setup.

## Enterprise fixtures

All golden fixtures run identically in CI regardless of tier — CI only needs committed plan.json, not Databricks auth. Enterprise tier affects regeneration only. Convention: enterprise fixtures under fixtures/golden/enterprise/<name>/. Not blocking this design.

## Budget estimate (500 min/month GitLab free tier)

At 60 feature pushes + 10 MR pushes + 8 main-merges + 2 tag releases per month:
- 60 × 45s ≈ 45 min
- 10 × 3 min ≈ 30 min
- 8 × 45s ≈ 6 min
- 2 × 2 min ≈ 4 min
Total: ~85 min/month. With interruptible collapsing bursts: realistically 60-70 min/month.

## What this bead does NOT specify
- Exact Playwright image tag → 21qe picks a specific pinned version
- Exact cache:key granularity → 39ue tunes during implementation
- Enterprise fixture introduction → future bead

title: Split create/delete diffs into per-field entries
description:
Redesign detail panel to show three clearly labeled sections for any node: Added (create), Modified (update), Unchanged.

## Added (create) — for new tasks or new fields on modified tasks
Instead of one giant JSON blob, break into individual field entries. Each field (depends_on, notebook_task, task_key) gets its own card with green + styling. For a fully new task, the header says "this task was created" and each field is shown as its own create entry.

## Modified (update) — for changed fields
Current behavior is good: shows old value with - red, new value with + green (structural diff). Additionally:
- If a key is REMOVED from a modified task (e.g. depends_on deleted entirely), show it in red with - as its own entry in this section
- If a key is ADDED to a modified task, show it in green with + as its own entry

## Unchanged — for fields that didn't change
Renamed from "Unmodified Configuration". Shows resource state fields not involved in any change, neutral styling.

## Removed (delete) — for deleted tasks or removed fields
Mirror of create: each field gets its own card in red with - styling. "This task was deleted, here's what it had."

## Section ordering
Added → Modified → Unchanged (or just the relevant ones depending on node diffState)

## Key files
- src/components/detail-panel.tsx (main rendering logic, ChangeEntry, ResourceStateView)
- src/utils/structural-diff.ts (computeStructuralDiff)
- src/components/structural-diff-view.tsx (StructuralDiffView)

Supersedes dagshund-jze.

title: refactor: eliminate ELK singleton module-level let
description: layout-graph.ts:16 uses let elkInstance at module scope as a lazy singleton, violating the project's 'no mutable module state' rule. Since elk.bundled.js is the non-worker version, new ELK() is cheap (the heavy 1.6MB module parse already happened at import). Fix: either create per-call (simplest), use a closure-based lazy init, or pass the instance as a parameter to toReactFlowElements/layoutResourceGraph.

title: fix: TS4111 bracket notation errors in generate-large-fixture.ts
Element-level depends_on changes produce missing/wrong edges
description:
## Bug

When a task's `depends_on` array changes elements without changing length (e.g. swapping one dependency for another), Databricks bundle plan records the change at the **element level** (`depends_on[0].task_key`) instead of the **whole-array level** (`depends_on`). Our edge-building code in `build-plan-graph.ts` only checks for the whole-array change key, so it misses the diff entirely.

### Result

- The old edge (e.g. `conditional_task → downstream`) is never created as "removed"
- The new edge (e.g. `load → downstream`) shows as "unchanged" instead of "added"
- Removed tasks that were only connected through these edges appear as isolated red nodes

### How Databricks records depends_on changes

**Array length changes** (e.g. 2 deps → 1 dep) — whole-array diff:
```json
"tasks[task_key='publish'].depends_on": {
  "old": [{"task_key": "full_validation"}, {"task_key": "skip_validation"}],
  "new": [{"task_key": "full_validation"}]
}
```
Our code handles this correctly.

**Array length stays the same** (e.g. 1 dep → 1 dep, just different target) — element-level diff:
```json
"tasks[task_key='full_validation'].depends_on[0].task_key": {
  "old": "check_row_count",
  "new": "load"
}
"tasks[task_key='full_validation'].depends_on[0].outcome": {
  "old": "true"
}
```
Our code never sees this — it only looks for `tasks[task_key='X'].depends_on`, not `depends_on[N].task_key`.

### Reproduction

Discovered by deploying a DAB with a `condition_task` (check_row_count) that branches to `full_validation` (true) and `skip_validation` (false), then removing the conditional and rewiring `full_validation` to depend on `load` directly. The plan JSON is saved in `unsanitized_fixtures/conditional-task-plan.json`.

### Affected code

`js/src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts` lines 142-163 — `buildEdgesForTask` constructs `dependsOnChangeKey` as `tasks[task_key='X'].depends_on` and only checks that single key. It needs to also handle element-level changes, or better: compare `remote_state.tasks` against `new_state.tasks` directly to find edge diffs regardless of how the plan records them.

### Fix direction

Compare the full set of dependencies from `remote_state.tasks` against `new_state.tasks` to find added/removed edges, rather than relying solely on the `changes` record format. This is format-agnostic and handles both array-level and element-level changes.
close_reason: Fixed — comparing remote_state directly for edge diffing

title: feat: show node counts in tab bar labels
description: R3-MED-14. The tab bar shows 'Resources' and 'Jobs' with no counts. Users can't tell if a tab is empty or how large the graph is. Fix: pass node counts from the plan data to TabBar and display as 'Resources (12)' / 'Jobs (3)'. When count is 0, show '(0)' to set expectations. The counts can be derived from the plan entries — jobs are entries matching resources.jobs.*, resources are everything else. Located in components/tab-bar.tsx and App.tsx where PlanView passes props. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Restructure Lakebase Provisioned: eliminate separate hierarchy, integrate into UC
description:
Restructure Lakebase Provisioned: remove separate hierarchy, integrate into UC.

## Current model (wrong)
Lakebase Provisioned has its own root node and hierarchy: instance → synced_table. This is wrong.

## Correct model
- **database_instance** = managed Postgres server (storage + compute + databases). Flat workspace resource, not a hierarchy container. Things reference it, it doesn't contain things.
- **database_catalog** = one Postgres database inside an instance, registered as a UC catalog. Sits at catalog-tier in UC. Fields: name (UC catalog name), database_instance_name (which instance), database_name (which Postgres DB).
- **synced_database_table** = read-only Postgres copy of a UC source table. Lives in UC under a catalog (database_catalog or standard). Three-part name: catalog.schema_or_database.table.

## Two paths for synced tables in UC
- **Database catalog path**: UC → database_catalog → ??? → synced_table
- **Standard catalog path**: UC → standard_catalog → schema → synced_table

## What needs to change
- Remove 'Lakebase Provisioned' root node and separate hierarchy
- database_catalog is a catalog-tier UC node (remove from Lakebase hierarchy)
- synced_database_tables belong in UC under their catalog
- database_instance is a flat workspace resource
- database_instance_name references become non-hierarchy dependency edges (dagshund-czfs)

## source_table_full_name edge (merged from dagshund-kkz8)
synced_database_tables have a spec.source_table_full_name field that references the UC table data is synced FROM. Currently we only model the destination side (parsing the name field as a three-part UC name for sync edges). The source_table_full_name is the actual data source.

Data flow: UC source table (source_table_full_name) → synced_database_table → appears at name (destination in UC)

After restructuring, synced_database_tables will live in the UC tree under their destination catalog. The source_table_full_name edge would connect from another UC table (possibly in a different catalog) to the synced table, showing the data flow across catalogs.

Example from docs:
  spec:
    source_table_full_name: 'source_catalog.schema.table'

Sync table data flow (from docs):
Source: UC table (Delta, Iceberg, views, materialized views) identified by source_table_full_name
Mechanism: managed Lakeflow Spark Declarative pipeline (auto schema mapping, continuous sync)
Destination: read-only Postgres table in Lakebase instance, registered in UC

The source_table_full_name edge represents the actual data flow: UC source → sync pipeline → synced table. This is the primary relationship to model. This is a non-hierarchy edge and should be part of the toggle (dagshund-czfs).

## Open question — NEEDS MANUAL TESTING AT WORK
Does a database_catalog have UC schemas under it? If so:
- UC schema under database_catalog = Postgres schema inside the database
- Full mapping: database_catalog(UC) = database(Postgres), schema(UC) = schema(Postgres), synced_table(UC) = table(Postgres)
- This would mean both paths use the same UC hierarchy: catalog → schema → synced_table

This needs to be verified by creating a database_catalog with a synced table in an actual Databricks workspace and checking whether schemas appear under the database_catalog in UC.

## Docs
- https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/oltp/instances/instance
- https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/oltp/instances/sync-data/sync-table
- https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/bundles/resources#database_catalogs

title: Add misc group node between workspace-root and flat resources when hierarchies present
description: When either Postgres or Lakebase hierarchies exist under workspace-root, the flat workspace resources (jobs, alerts, external_locations, etc.) sit at the same level as postgres-root and lakebase-root, which can make the layout less clear. Add a phantom 'Misc' (or similar) group node: workspace-root → misc-root → flat resources. Only create this grouping when at least one hierarchy (postgres or lakebase) is also present — when everything is flat, no extra nesting needed. This should help ELK produce cleaner layering by giving flat resources their own sub-section.
close_reason: Implemented other-resources-root grouping with conditional wrapping

title: Render create-only diffs with per-field object-diff layout
description: When a create-only diff value is a plain object, render it using the same per-key layout as the object diff view (each top-level key on its own line with + prefix and green styling) instead of dumping the raw JSON blob. This makes creates visually consistent with modifications without splitting them into separate change entries. The section header still shows the single create action — only the value rendering changes.
close_reason: Superseded by dagshund-icm with updated spec — user wants per-field entries for create/delete diffs, not just per-key rendering under one header

title: test_terminal.py: relocate format.py tests, drop golden-duplicate integration tests
description:
`test_terminal.py` is 2152 lines / 178 tests. The number is misleading — most of it is legitimate pure-function unit coverage. The real issues are organizational drift and a handful of tests that duplicate the golden harness.

## Part A — Drop golden-duplicate integration tests

A small set of `test_render_text_*` tests load a golden plan.json, render with default flags, and assert on output substrings. `generate_expected.sh --check` already does this end-to-end with whole-file diff against `expected.txt` / `expected.md` / `expected-exit.txt`, at higher fidelity. Pure dual-feed — delete.

Candidates (verify by rereading before removing):
- `test_render_text_real_fixture`
- `test_render_text_mixed_plan_fixture`
- `test_render_text_sample_plan_fixture`
- `test_render_text_invalid_plan_fixture`

**Keep** integration tests that exercise something golden doesn't:
- `--changes-only`, `-a`, `-r` flag behavior (would need a new golden fixture per flag combo — net maintenance goes up)
- Color on/off (golden uses `NO_COLOR=1`)
- Error paths (`render_text_empty_plan_raises_error`, `render_text_missing_plan_key_raises_error`)
- Sub-resource merge behavior (`test_render_text_merges_sub_resources`)

## Part B — Move format.py tests to test_format.py

Organizational drift: tests for functions defined in `format.py` live in `test_terminal.py` because `terminal.py` re-imports them. `test_format.py` exists (183 lines) and is sparsely populated.

Move (approximate — audit during the cut):
- `format_value`, `format_transition`, `is_long_string`, `format_display_value`
- `_extract_drift_label_noun`, `detect_drift_reentries`
- `detect_drift_fields`, `_summarize_resource_drift`, `collect_drift_summaries`
- `collect_warnings`
- `iter_non_topology_field_changes`
- `count_by_action`, `group_by_resource_type`
- `filter_resources`, `format_group_header`
- `action_config`, `action_to_diff_state`
- `parse_resource_key` (currently in test_terminal, belongs in test_types or test_format)

**Keep in test_terminal.py:**
- `_supports_color`, `_colorize`, `_detect_terminal_width`, `_wrap_transition`, `_wrap_warning_line`
- `_render_field_change`, `_render_resource`, `_print_header`, `_print_resource_groups`, `_print_summary`, `_print_warnings`, `_print_drift_warnings`, `_iter_drift_warning_lines`
- `render_text` integration tests (the ones kept from Part A)

## Expected outcome

- `test_terminal.py` drops to ~800–1000 lines, scope narrows to terminal-specific rendering + I/O
- `test_format.py` grows proportionally
- Net: same tests, organized by the module they test
- No coverage regression — `just check` including `test-golden` still passes

## Non-goals

- Not migrating unit tests to fixtures — those give pinpoint failure messages the golden run can't match.
- Not deduping between `test_terminal.py` and `test_markdown.py` — they test different renderers even when the underlying logic is shared; each side has a legitimate reason to verify.

title: buildResourceGraph growing — extract flat phantom pattern
description:
collectPhantomDatabaseInstances is the first flat phantom type created outside the chain spec system. If a second flat phantom type appears (e.g. phantom warehouses, phantom models), extract a generic collectFlatPhantoms pattern. Not worth doing for a single case.

title: app→job shows duplicate normal + lateral edge
description:
In the app fixture, both a normal edge (job → app) and a lateral edge (app → job) appear between the app and the job it depends on. Opposite directions, same node pair, so deduplicateEdges (which keys on direction-aware edge IDs) doesn't catch it.

## Root cause

Two extractors fire independently for the same pair:

1. collectDependsOnEdges (build-resource-graph.ts:577) reads the app's `depends_on` array and emits `job → app`.
2. createAppResourcesSpec (extract-lateral-edges.ts:238) reads the app's `state.resources[]` and emits `app → job`.

Warehouses don't hit this because DAB only puts warehouses in `state.resources[]`, not in `depends_on` — only the lateral edge gets created.

## Fix direction: suppress the lateral when a depends_on covers the same pair

The depends_on edge is strictly more informative:
- Lateral edges are hardcoded `\"unchanged\"` (extract-lateral-edges.ts:122 — `buildGraphEdge(..., \"unchanged\", \"lateral::\")`).
- Depends_on edges carry the real diff state via `resolveEntryEdgeDiffState(entry)` (build-resource-graph.ts:584).

So depends_on wins; the lateral was only ever a fallback for resource kinds the CLI doesn't emit a depends_on for (warehouses, secrets, etc).

## Implementation sketch

1. In buildResourceGraph, after collectDependsOnEdges, build a `Set<string>` of unordered node-pair keys: `[a, b].sort().join(\"→\")`.
2. Pass it into extractLateralEdges via the LateralEdgeContext.
3. In applyLateralEdgeSpec, skip a lateral edge if its unordered pair is already in the set.

## Repro

Load fixtures/golden/app-dependencies/plan.json and observe the app→job connection vs the warehouse→job connection.

## Follow-up after fix

Regenerate goldens for fixtures/golden/app-dependencies and any other fixture exercising app→job lateral edges. Run \`just gen-expected app-dependencies\` (or --all), review diff, commit.

title: Fix dagre/graphlib dynamic require incompatibility with Bun bundler
description: dagre v2 ESM bundle wraps a CJS require('@dagrejs/graphlib') in a compat shim. Bun serve() bundler cannot trace this dynamic require at bundle time, causing a runtime error in the browser. Fix: pre-bundle dagre+graphlib into a single self-contained ESM file using bun build, then import that from layout-graph.ts.
close_reason: Vendored dagre CJS as ESM; build/tests/types all pass

title: GitHub mirror not indexed by Claude marketplace
description:
The claudemarketplaces.com scanner requires **5+ GitHub stars** to index a repo. Our mirror (github.com/chinchyisbored/dagshund) has 0 stars, so it's filtered out before the scanner even fetches files.

Our plugin infrastructure is valid — marketplace.json schema passes, plugin.json is correct, SKILL.md has proper frontmatter.

Secondary issue: the skill discovery path searches for `filename:SKILL.md path:.claude/skills`, but our SKILL.md lives at `plugins/dagshund/skills/dagshund/SKILL.md`. This only affects the separate skills index, not the marketplace index.

**Blockers:**
1. Need 5+ stars on the GitHub mirror (hard gate, no workaround)
2. (Optional) Add a .claude/skills symlink or copy for skill-specific discovery

**No code changes needed** — this is a community/adoption problem, not a bug.

title: Job container node + left-to-right DAG layout
description:
Transform the current flat node layout into a Databricks-style DAG:

1. **Job as group/container node**: Use React Flow's group node (parentId) so the job is a labeled container with task nodes rendered inside it. The job node should show the job name as a header and have a border styled by its diff state.

2. **Left-to-right ELK layout**: Change `elk.direction` from `DOWN` to `RIGHT`. Tune spacing so the primary dependency chain flows horizontally and branching/parallel tasks fan vertically.

3. **Smooth curved edges**: Use React Flow's `smoothstep` or `bezier` edge type instead of default straight edges.

4. **Linear preference**: Configure ELK to prefer linear/straight paths for the longest chain, pushing branches to the side.

### Files to modify
- `src/graph/layout-graph.ts` — flip direction, add group node support, adjust spacing
- `src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts` — set parentId on task nodes pointing to job node
- `src/components/job-node.tsx` — restyle as container (larger, acts as boundary)
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` — update handle positions (Left/Right instead of Top/Bottom)
- `src/App.tsx` — update edge options for smooth curves
- Tests need updating for new layout direction and group node structure

### Reference
Databricks job overview DAG: left-to-right flow, linear spine, vertical branching.
close_reason: Job container + LTR layout complete. Lazy ELK init fixes browser rendering.

title: refactor: consolidate task-key prefix pattern across codebase
description: The tasks[task_key='...'] pattern is constructed/matched independently in 5 files: (1) TASK_KEY_PATTERN regex in build-task-change-summary.ts (2) WHOLE_TASK_KEY_PATTERN regex in extract-tasks.ts (3) TASK_KEY_PREFIX_PATTERN regex in detail-panel.tsx (4) Template string in build-plan-graph.ts (lines 70, 106, 145) (5) Template string in resolve-task-diff-state.ts (lines 13, 47). Also filterTaskChanges and collectChangesForTask are the same filter with different return types. Fix: single buildTaskKeyPrefix() helper, one shared regex, consolidate the two filter functions.
