title: feat: slide animation on detail panel open/close
description: Detail panel pops in/out instantly — 390px layout shift is jarring. Fix: use CSS transition from translate-x-full to translate-x-0 with 150-200ms ease-out. Or overlay the panel with position:absolute and subtle backdrop.

title: Inline _check_all_unchanged at its single call site
description: _check_all_unchanged in text.py:220-222 is a trivial wrapper that returns 'not detect_changes(resources)'. Called exactly once at line 238. Inline it as 'if not detect_changes(resources):' to remove unnecessary indirection.

title: V1 Vision: Databricks-style plan visualizer
description:
## Vision

Dagshund V1 is a local tool that accepts piped `databricks bundle plan -o json` output, opens a browser window, and shows the plan as an interactive DAG with diff highlighting.

Eventually this becomes a VS Code plugin, but V1 is browser-based.

### Visual Model (inspired by Databricks job DAG UI)

- **Job as container**: Each job is a labeled container/group node. Tasks live inside it.
- **Tasks as DAG**: Tasks are rounded-rectangle cards connected by smooth curved edges showing dependency relationships.
- **Left-to-right layout**: The primary dependency chain flows horizontally left→right. Branching/parallel tasks fan vertically (up/down) off the main spine. Prefer linear edge paths.
- **Reference**: Databricks job overview UI — see `/tmp/databricks-dag.png` or https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/assets/images/example-job-overview-3afc40004602e65c871bd67edfa87f9b.png

### Diff Color Coding

- **Green** (border + subtle bg): New component (added)
- **Yellow/Amber** (border + subtle bg): Changed component (modified)
- **Red + reduced opacity (40%)**: Deleted component (removed)
- **Neutral**: Unchanged component

### Interaction Model

- **Click task** → Right-side slide-in detail panel showing all changes for that task
- **Hover task** → Visual highlighting of the task and its immediate dependency edges
- Pan, zoom, minimap for navigation

### Data Flow

```
databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund
  → Bun server reads stdin, serves frontend
  → Frontend fetches /api/plan
  → Zod parse + validate
  → Transform to graph model
  → ELK layout (left-to-right, layered)
  → React Flow render with custom nodes
```

### What's Done

- Stdin pipe → server → browser auto-open
- Zod parsing and validation
- Graph model (Plan → nodes + edges)
- Diff state resolution and color coding
- ELK layout (currently top-to-bottom, needs flip)
- React Flow rendering with custom job/task nodes
- Tests (51 passing, 127 assertions)

### What's Remaining for V1

1. Job as container node (React Flow group/parent)
2. Left-to-right layout with linear preference
3. Click task → detail panel
4. Hover task → highlighting
close_reason: V1 feature set complete: DAG visualization, diff highlighting, detail panel, ELK layout, hover interactions all shipped

title: fix: add focus-visible rings to all interactive elements
description: Close button, format toggle, disclosure button, and filter buttons have no focus:ring styles. Keyboard users can't see where focus is. Fix: add focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent to all buttons. Consider a shared button style utility.

title: Center-align nodes on vertical layer grid to eliminate edge backtracking
description: Nodes in the same DAG layer should share the same x-coordinate, forming clean vertical columns. All task nodes are already equal width (200px), so alignment mode (left vs center) is irrelevant — the issue is whether ELK is actually snapping same-layer nodes to the same x. Investigate whether the current ELK config already does this correctly and if not, configure elk.layered.nodePlacement to enforce it. The goal: fan-out/fan-in edges flow cleanly right then split/merge vertically — no S-curves or backtracking wraps.

title: fix: bind dev server to localhost (127.0.0.1)
description: Server in src/index.ts binds to 0.0.0.0 (Bun default), exposing plan data to the local network. Databricks plan JSON may contain sensitive config. Fix: add hostname: '127.0.0.1' to serve() call. One-line change.

title: refactor: replace as assertions in build-resource-graph with Zod parsing
description: build-resource-graph.ts has 6 'as' assertions to narrow z.unknown() fields (new_state, remote_state) without comments. extract-tasks.ts already uses Zod safeParse for the same pattern. Fix: define Zod schemas for resource state shapes and use safeParse instead of as casts. Aligns with 'parse, don't validate' from CLAUDE.md.

title: Consider caching _parse_resource_key results per resource
description: _parse_resource_key is called ~3 times per resource: once in sorted(), once in groupby(), and once in _render_resource(). Each call does str.split('.') and pattern matching. Trivially cheap at current scale but redundant work. Low priority — only worth addressing if resource counts grow significantly.
close_reason: Won't fix: premature optimization that would also introduce module-level mutable state via @cache, violating code philosophy

title: Single source of truth for action vocabulary across Python and TS
description:
The plan action vocabulary ('create', 'delete', 'update', 'recreate', 'resize', 'update_id', 'skip', '') is currently duplicated in three places:

- src/dagshund/plan.py::action_to_diff_state — match statement
- src/dagshund/format.py::ACTIONS — display config dict (symbol, label, show_field_changes)
- js/src/types/plan-schema.ts::knownActionTypes — Zod enum

plan.py:36 has a comment warning about this. It's a ticking bomb: adding an action means updating three files and hoping no one forgets.

Proposal: move to src/dagshund/_assets/actions.json as source of truth.

  {
    "create":    { "diff": "added",     "symbol": "+" },
    "delete":    { "diff": "removed",   "symbol": "-" },
    "update":    { "diff": "modified",  "symbol": "~", "show_field_changes": true },
    "recreate":  { "diff": "modified",  "symbol": "~", "show_field_changes": true },
    "resize":    { "diff": "modified",  "symbol": "~", "show_field_changes": true },
    "update_id": { "diff": "modified",  "symbol": "~", "show_field_changes": true },
    "":          { "diff": "unchanged", "symbol": "=" },
    "skip":      { "diff": "unchanged", "symbol": "=" }
  }

Loading:
- Python: importlib.resources at import time in plan.py / format.py, build the ACTIONS dict + StrEnum from it
- TS: Bun supports `import actions from '...actions.json'` directly; derive knownActionTypes from Object.keys()

Parity test:
- tests/test_cross_language.py asserts the JSON keys match a snapshot that also gets checked in TS tests. If Databricks adds an action and only one side updates, CI fails.

Should be generalized: there's a second cross-language contract at src/dagshund/browser.py::_escape_for_script_tag (which has a comment 'Keep in sync with escapeJsonForScript() in js/src/html-assembler.ts'). Same parity-test pattern applies — encode an identical fixture string on both sides, assert byte-equal output. File as a follow-up or include in this bead's scope.

Cost: small. Maybe 150 lines of diff total including the parity test. Highest ROI item on the cleanup list — depends on nothing, unblocks nothing, just kills a whole class of drift bugs.

title: Add missing resource type badges
description: Add TYPE_BADGES entries in resource-node.tsx for 8 missing resource types: database_instance, model, postgres_project, postgres_branch, postgres_endpoint, quality_monitor, synced_database_table, external_locations. Trivial — cosmetic only, no logic changes.

title: feat: plan-level summary header with change counts
description: No summary of plan contents when loaded. Users need 'how big is this change?' at a glance. Fix: add compact summary bar below tab bar showing counts like '3 jobs | 12 tasks | +2 ~3 -1' using diff state colors for the numbers.
close_reason: Deferred — need to decide on placement. Filter buttons already show diff counts; the summary (job/task/resource totals) needs a home that doesn't fight with the detail panel or clutter the canvas.

title: refactor: extract shared HTML assembler from cli.ts, build-template.ts, and index.html
description: R3-HIGH-1. The HTML assembly logic is triplicated across three files: js/src/cli.ts (assembleHtml), js/scripts/build-template.ts (assembleTemplate), and js/src/index.html (theme init script). Functions duplicated: runBuild(), readDistAssets(), escapeForScriptTag(), escapeForStyleTag(), plus the HTML template literal itself. A security fix to escape logic in one file that misses the others is a real risk. Extract a shared js/src/html-assembler.ts module with assembleHtml(css, js, planSlot) that both cli.ts and build-template.ts import. The theme init script should live in one place. Also extract detectOpenCommand/tryOpenBrowser into utils/open-browser.ts (currently copy-pasted between index.ts and cli.ts). Flagged by: Architect (HIGH), Code Quality (CRITICAL).

title: Add host validation to dev server static route
description: js/src/index.ts:40-52 — the /api/plan endpoint checks isAllowedHost(request) for DNS rebinding protection, but the /* catch-all static route does not. Low risk since static assets contain no secrets and the data endpoint is protected, but adding the check to /* would be consistent.

title: refactor: eliminate type drift and naming inconsistencies
description: Multiple type/naming inconsistencies: (1) DagNodeData (graph-types.ts:42-52) manually copies GraphNode minus id — should be Omit<GraphNode,'id'>. (2) Same 9-field data spreading written 3x in layout-graph.ts — extract toNodeData helper. (3) LayoutResult type defined identically in use-plan-graph.ts and use-resource-graph.ts — share one definition. (4) formatJobLabel vs extractResourceName — identical last-dot-segment logic with different names. (5) extractTypeBadge vs extractResourceType — same key extraction, different names. (6) getEdgeStyle uses inline literal type instead of EdgeDiffState. (7) EdgeDiffState not exported from types barrel. (8) 'changed' vs 'modified' for the same concept across structural-diff vs graph types.

title: Break up dense comprehension in _group_by_resource_type
description: text.py:188 has a 103-char one-liner chaining groupby with dict() inside a dict comprehension. Split into two statements for readability: assign grouped = groupby(sorted_entries, key=_resource_type_of) first, then build the dict comprehension from it.

title: Consider splitting buildHierarchySubgraph into smaller composable functions
description:
Three related issues from review:

1. Missing nodeIds.has(sourceNodeId) guard in extractDatabaseInstanceEdges and extractServingEndpointModelEdges — inconsistent with extractWarehouseEdges which does the check. Low risk since nodeIdByResourceKey is built from allNodes, but should be consistent.

2. Lateral edge IDs use the same source→target format as hierarchy edges. If a lateral edge coincides with a depends_on or hierarchy edge, duplicate IDs would confuse React Flow. Prefix lateral IDs with 'lateral::' to guarantee uniqueness.

3. extractPipelineTargetEdges could emit duplicate edges for the same pipeline→schema pair if ingestion_definition.objects has redundant entries. Add a seen-set dedup within the function.
close_reason: All three issues already fixed: source node guards present in all extractors, lateral:: prefix in buildLateralEdge, seen-set dedup in extractPipelineTargetEdges. Tests cover all three.

title: fix: harden script tag escaping with universal < replacement
description: R3-MED-24. _escape_for_script_tag in browser.py and escapeForScriptTag in cli.ts/build-template.ts only escape </script and <\!-- patterns. The industry-standard approach is simpler and comprehensive: replace ALL < with the unicode escape. Python: content.replace('<', '\\u003c'). JS: content.replace(/</g, '\\u003c'). Current escaping IS functional (prevents </script tag closure), but the universal approach is defense-in-depth with fewer edge cases. This fix should be made in the shared html-assembler.ts module (see R3-HIGH-1) and in browser.py. Flagged by: Security/Perf (LOW x3), but promoted to MEDIUM because the fix is trivial and eliminates an entire class of injection vectors.

title: Render ghost nodes and ghost edges for deleted tasks and changed dependencies
description:
Two related gaps in DAG visualization, implemented together:

**Ghost nodes (deleted tasks):** Tasks that are deleted (exist in changes record with action=delete but not in new_state) currently don't appear as nodes in the DAG. They should be rendered as red nodes with reduced opacity (removed diff state styling). Requires extracting deleted task keys from the changes record in buildEntryGraph and creating ghost nodes for them.

**Ghost edges (changed dependencies):** When a modified task changes its depends_on array, removed edges just disappear and new edges appear gray/unchanged. Example: task A depends_on [B, C] → [B, D]. Edge A→C should render red/removed, edge A→D should render green/added. Requires comparing old vs new depends_on from changes record, creating ghost edges for removed deps, and marking new edges as added. GraphEdge needs a diffState field.

title: Design and implement CI/CD pipeline
description:
Design and implement GitLab CI/CD pipeline for dagshund.

## Current State
- .gitlab-ci.yml exists with SAST + secret detection on every push
- No linting, typechecking, testing, or build verification in CI
- No release/publish pipeline
- 400 CI minutes/month (GitLab free tier)

## Open Questions

### Pipeline structure
Two trigger points discussed:
1. Every push to main — security only (already have)
2. On tags (releases) — full pipeline

But adding HTML export testing (Playwright) changes the picture — Playwright needs a browser runtime, which is heavier. Do we:
- Run Playwright only on tags?
- Run a lighter HTML validation (file exists, correct structure) on every push, Playwright only on tags?
- Skip the per-push security and only run everything on tags to save minutes?

### HTML export testing
Want to verify the HTML export actually works, not just that it builds. Options discussed:
1. Basic: generate HTML from fixture, check it's non-empty and contains expected markers
2. Middle ground: parse HTML in Bun, verify script tags and embedded data
3. Playwright: headless browser, check no JS errors, DOM elements render, click interactions work

Playwright is the real acceptance test but adds complexity:
- Needs playwright install (downloads browsers)
- Heavier Docker image or extra CI step
- More CI minutes per run
- But only runs on tags, so maybe acceptable

### Release cycle
- PyPI publish step needed (placeholder for now)
- Manual trigger vs automatic on tag push
- Need to set up PyPI trusted publisher or API token

### Minute budget
- 400 min/month on GitLab free tier
- Security scan per push is cheap
- Full pipeline (lint + typecheck + test + build + Playwright) per release — how expensive?
- User is sole contributor, runs just check locally before every commit

title: Job sorting for large bundle files
description: Large bundle plans with many jobs render in arbitrary order, making it hard to find specific jobs. Add sorting options (alphabetical, by diff state, by task count) to the DAG view so users can orient themselves quickly in big graphs.

title: explore shared action vocabulary across Python + TS
description:
## Status: CLOSED — won't-fix (2026-04-15)

Originally filed as the "#1 systemic reliability risk" — action vocabulary duplicated across 5 sites (plan.py, format.py, plan-schema.ts, map-diff-state.ts, field-action.ts) with no parity enforcement.

## Rationale for closing

Deliberated three approaches:

1. **Shared actions.json in wheel + cross-tree TS import** (original proposal) — couples JS build to Python tree layout. Rejected as over-engineered.

2. **Parity test via committed fixtures/action-vocab.json** — would work, ~3 small files plus a hand-edited 8-line JSON contract. Drafted in full; see `~/.claude/plans/eager-booping-starfish.md`.

3. **Do nothing** — chosen.

## Why do-nothing won

- Vocabulary has 8 actions and has been stable for years. Databricks-owned, rarely extended.
- Solo maintainer. The forcing function the parity test adds (CI failure if one side is updated without the other) has near-zero value when one person is holding the mental model.
- No drift incident has ever been observed in practice.
- Any added scaffolding (even the minimum: one JSON + two tests) is ceremony relative to the probability of the problem recurring.

## When to revisit

- A second contributor starts touching the action code paths regularly.
- Databricks adds a new action and we actually experience drift between Python and TS rendering.
- Either of: (a) `field-action.ts` grows materially, (b) a third consumer of the vocabulary appears.

## Original proposal (preserved for context)

The action vocabulary is hand-maintained in three places today:
- js/src/types/plan-schema.ts (Zod knownActionTypes)
- src/dagshund/plan.py (action_to_diff_state match)
- src/dagshund/format.py (ACTIONS dict — show_field_changes, labels, etc.)

(Note: exploration during planning found two more sites — js/src/parser/map-diff-state.ts and js/src/utils/field-action.ts — so the real drift surface was 5 files, not 3.)

Shared JSON source of truth was proposed, with cross-tree imports from js/src/ into the Python package. Rejected in favor of do-nothing per the reasoning above.
close_reason: won't-fix: solo codebase + stable 8-entry vocab + no observed drift = parity scaffolding is ceremony. Revisit if a second contributor appears or real drift is observed. Full rationale in description.

title: Array diff marks modified identity-matched elements as unchanged
description:
In structural-diff.ts:132-139, when diffArrays matches elements by identity key (e.g. task_key), matched elements are always marked status: 'unchanged' regardless of whether their other fields differ. The ArrayElement type only supports 'added' | 'removed' | 'unchanged' — no 'modified' variant.

However, this may not be reachable in practice: Databricks emits changes at the specific field path level (e.g. tasks[task_key='transform'].notebook_task.notebook_path), not as whole-array comparisons. The structural diff only sees the scalar old/new values from individual change records, not full array diffs. The bug would only trigger if a change record's old/new values were both arrays containing identity-keyed objects with differing sub-fields.

Needs investigation: find or construct a real plan where a change record contains array-typed old/new values with identity-matched elements that differ. If no such case exists in practice, downgrade to won't-fix.

title: Clean up action constants in text.py
description: The action-related constants at module level in src/dagshund/text.py are scattered and their relationship is implicit. UPDATE_ACTIONS is public (no underscore) while the related _DISPLAY_ACTIONS, _ACTION_STYLES, and _DEFAULT_STYLE are private. The dict.fromkeys spread in _ACTION_STYLES is clever but not immediately readable. Consider: (1) making UPDATE_ACTIONS private (_UPDATE_ACTIONS) since it's only used within the module, (2) grouping all action constants together with a clear comment block, (3) possibly replacing the dict.fromkeys spread with something more explicit.
notes: UPDATE_ACTIONS is imported in tests/test_text.py (line 13) for parametrized test generation. No other production code uses it. Making it private (_UPDATE_ACTIONS) would require updating the test import but is otherwise safe.
close_reason: Consolidated into _ActionConfig dataclass + _ACTIONS table. Fixed _print_summary round-trip bug.

title: Replace group nodes with generic chain spec and phantom traversal
description:
Refactor the resource graph node model and hierarchy building logic.

## Current problems
1. 'resource-group' nodeKind conflates three roles: root anchors, hierarchy containers, and phantom nodes
2. Container resources (postgres_projects, database_instances) render as both a group node AND a resource node (duplication bug)
3. Phantom creation is scattered across 5+ code paths, each special-cased per hierarchy
4. Phantom chains don't fully walk up (phantom branch skips to root instead of creating phantom project)
5. HierarchySpec only supports one mid-tier, can't extend to deeper chains

## New design

### Node kinds
Replace 'resource-group' with clean taxonomy:
- resource: anything from the plan (including containers)
- root: hierarchy anchors (UC, Workspace) — structural only
- phantom: inferred nodes to complete hierarchy chains

### Chain spec
Generic tier-based hierarchy definition:
- Each hierarchy is an ordered list of TierSpecs (root-adjacent → leaf)
- Each tier defines: resourceTypes, resolveIdentity, resolveParentRef, deriveParentRef, phantomId
- One generic traversal function walks up from any resource, creating phantoms for missing ancestors
- Replaces all scattered phantom logic with a single mechanism

### What changes
- New types: TierSpec, ChainSpec (in graph-types.ts)
- New function: generic chain traversal (replaces buildHierarchySubgraph, buildPhantomSchemaNodes, sync phantom logic)
- Remove 'resource-group' nodeKind entirely
- Update React components: ResourceGroupNode → split into RootNode + PhantomNode (or unified with ResourceNode)
- Update detail panel to handle new node kinds
- Update all tests

## Out of scope
- Reworking specific hierarchy definitions (Postgres/Lakebase relationships) — separate beads
- Cross-hierarchy reference edges (dagshund-czfs)
- The dev CSS fix (separate commit)
close_reason: Replaced resource-group with root/phantom nodeKinds. Generic chain spec traversal for phantom creation. Container dedup eliminates duplicate group+resource nodes.

title: refactor: extract shared FlowCanvas from DagView and ResourcesView
description: DagView (App.tsx) and ResourcesView (resources-view.tsx) share ~120 lines of near-identical code: state management, event handlers (handleNodeClick, handleClosePanel, handleNodeMouseEnter/Leave, handleInit), edge styling memo, hover/filter memos, buildConnectedNodeIds, EMPTY_NODES/EDGES, DEFAULT_EDGE_OPTIONS. Extract a shared FlowCanvas component that accepts layout result and nodeTypes as props. Both views become thin wrappers calling their respective hooks.

title: fix: add hover timer cleanup on FlowCanvas unmount
description: R3-LOW-27. The hover debounce timer (hoverTimerRef) in flow-canvas.tsx is cleared when new hover events arrive but has no cleanup on component unmount. In React 19 this is a no-op (setState on unmounted is silent), but it's technically imprecise. Fix: add useEffect(() => () => { if (hoverTimerRef.current \!== null) clearTimeout(hoverTimerRef.current) }, []). Located around line 66 of flow-canvas.tsx. Flagged by: Security/Perf (LOW).

title: Add multi-agent staff engineer code review
description: Set up a code review workflow using multiple high-experience staff engineer subagents. Each agent reviews the codebase from a different angle (architecture, correctness, performance, style, etc.) and provides feedback.
close_reason: Code review complete. 13 issues filed from 3 parallel specialist reviews.

title: refactor: split buildEdgesForTask into smaller functions
description: R3-LOW-32. buildEdgesForTask in build-plan-graph.ts:118-178 is 55 lines with 4 branches, each containing a .map((dep) => ({...})) with minor variations. Fix: extract a buildEdgeFromDep(resourceKey, depKey, taskKey, diffState) helper that creates a single GraphEdge, then each branch just maps deps through it. Reduces duplication and makes each case clearer. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: Extract shared _parse_plan() from text.py and browser.py
description: Both text.py and browser.py have identical _parse_plan() functions with 'keep in sync' comments. Extract to a shared module (e.g. __init__.py or _parse.py) and import in both places. Eliminates duplication and the risk of them drifting apart.

title: Decompose oversized functions and replace mutation with functional patterns
description:
## Problem

Four functions exceed the 20-line guideline, and three functions use imperative mutation (list.append, dict.setdefault) instead of functional patterns (generators, comprehensions). These overlap — the oversized functions are often the ones with mutation.

## Oversized Functions

### 1. `_render_resource` — ~34 logic lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 93-132
- Handles both the resource header AND field-level change details
- **Fix**: Extract field-change rendering into a separate function (e.g., `_render_field_changes`)
- Also uses `list.append()` mutation — replace with `yield` (make it a generator) or build via comprehension

### 2. `build_parser` — ~29 lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/cli.py`, around lines 18-47
- Largely declarative argparse configuration, so complexity is low
- **Fix**: Could extract argument groups into helper functions, but this is low priority given it's purely declarative

### 3. `main` — ~26 lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/cli.py`, around lines 72-97
- Handles argument parsing, branching (browser vs text), error handling, exit
- **Fix**: Extract browser branch (lines ~82-90) and text branch (lines ~91-94) into separate functions

### 4. `render_text` — borderline at ~21 lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 197-219
- Right at the limit, may not need changes after other refactoring

## Mutation Patterns to Replace

### 1. `_render_resource` — `lines.append()`
- Builds a `list[str]` via repeated `.append()` calls across ~30 lines
- **Fix**: Convert to a generator that `yield`s lines, or build via comprehension/concatenation

### 2. `_group_by_resource_type` — `dict.setdefault().append()`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 156-162
- Builds a dict of lists imperatively
- **Fix**: Use `itertools.groupby` on sorted input, or a comprehension-based approach

### 3. `_print_summary` — `summary_parts.append()`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 182-189
- Builds a list then joins it
- **Fix**: Replace with a generator expression inside `", ".join(...)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- No function in `src/dagshund/` exceeds 20 lines of logic
- No `list.append()` or `dict.setdefault()` mutation patterns — use generators, comprehensions, or `itertools`
- All existing tests pass without modification
- `just check` passes
close_reason: All mutation patterns replaced, all functions under 20 lines, pattern matching for type dispatch

title: Render DAG with diff-state colored nodes in React Flow
description:
Wire the graph model into React Flow rendering with visual diff-state distinction. Minimal styling — just enough to tell states apart.

DEPENDS ON: dagshund-ebk (graph model must exist first)

DATA CONTEXT:
The graph model produces three kinds of nodes:
- Job nodes (nodeKind='job'): represent the resource itself, contain metadata (name, triggers, compute)
- Task nodes (nodeKind='task'): individual tasks within a job, form the DAG via depends_on edges
- The user wants to see BOTH: the job as context/container and the tasks as the interactive DAG

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Custom React Flow node components:
   - src/components/task-node.tsx — renders a task node with diff-state color coding:
     - added: green border/accent
     - removed: red border, reduced opacity (40%)
     - modified: amber/yellow accent
     - unchanged: neutral/default (zinc/gray)
   - src/components/job-node.tsx — renders job metadata (name, etc.) as a header/container node
   Keep it simple — colored border or background tint is enough for now. No fancy visuals yet.

2. Update App.tsx to:
   - Accept a Plan object as prop or via context
   - Call graph model functions to get React Flow nodes/edges
   - Pass custom node types to ReactFlow
   - For now, use a hardcoded test plan or accept via window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ global (stdin feature will wire this up)

3. Custom hook (src/hooks/use-plan-graph.ts):
   - Takes Plan, returns { nodes, edges } ready for React Flow
   - Memoized with useMemo

CONSTRAINTS:
- Function components only, no classes
- All readonly where possible
- Register custom node types properly with React Flow nodeTypes (must be stable reference, not inline object)
- Must handle empty plan gracefully (no nodes = empty canvas, not a crash)
- Dark background (zinc-950 already set in App.tsx) — colors should work on dark
- Handle multiple jobs producing separate sub-graphs within the same canvas
close_reason: Custom job/task nodes with diff-state coloring, usePlanGraph hook, App wired with mixed-plan fixture. 51 tests passing, tsc clean.

title: Support detailed exit codes for CI usage (like terraform plan)
description:
Support detailed exit codes for CI usage, similar to terraform plan -detailed-exitcode.

## Exit codes (opt-in via flag)
- Exit 0: plan parsed, no changes (all resources have action 'skip')
- Exit 1: error (bad input, missing file, etc.)
- Exit 2: plan parsed, changes detected (at least one resource has a non-skip action)

## Key discovery
Databricks includes ALL managed resources in plan output, even unchanged ones. Unchanged resources have `"action": "skip"` at the top level. So `plan["plan"]` being non-empty does NOT mean drift.

Drift detection logic:
```python
has_changes = any(
    entry.get("action") not in ("skip", "")
    for entry in plan.values()
)
```

## Fixture
`fixtures/no-changes-plan.json` — real plan output where nothing changed. All 8 resources have `"action": "skip"`.

## Open questions
- Flag name: --detailed-exitcode (terraform convention) vs shorter alternative
- Applies to both text and browser modes
- Interaction with dagshund-cfx: empty plan (no plan key / empty dict) is still exit 1 (error). A plan full of skips is exit 0 (success, no changes). These are different situations.

title: refactor: replace typing.Any with object in text.py _format_value
description: R3-MED-19. text.py imports typing.Any and uses it in _format_value(value: Any). Python's object type is the equivalent of TypeScript's unknown — requires explicit narrowing before use. Fix: change 'from typing import Any' to remove the import, change parameter to _format_value(value: object) -> str. The isinstance checks inside already narrow correctly. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: Add tests for pure utility functions: format-value, resource-key, task-key
description:
Three pure utility modules have no direct test files:

1. **format-value.ts**: Three code paths (undefined → '<absent>', yaml format, json format), used by 6 components. Test all paths including null literal and trimEnd behavior.

2. **resource-key.ts**: extractResourceName splits on '.' and returns last segment with a ?? fallback. Test normal dotted keys, single segment, and empty string edge case.

3. **task-key.ts**: Exports regex patterns (TASK_KEY_PATTERN, TASK_KEY_DOT_PREFIX_PATTERN) and helper functions (filterJobLevelChanges, collectChangesForTask). Currently covered transitively but a regex change could silently break things. Add direct unit tests.

title: Add postgres and database hierarchy to resource graph

Add parent-child edges in build-resource-graph.ts for two new hierarchies:

1. Postgres: postgres_project → postgres_branch → postgres_endpoint (linked via parent field)
2. Database: database_instance → database_catalog (via database_instance_name) and database_instance → synced_database_table (via database_instance_name)

Similar pattern to existing UC hierarchy (catalog → schema → leaf). Needs new tier sets, hierarchy edge building, and possibly phantom nodes for missing parents.

The AppResource schema allows three additional reference types not yet handled:

1. genie_space — has space_id. genie_spaces is not currently a bundle resource type but could appear as a reference target.
2. database — has instance_name, database_name, permission. Marked x-databricks-preview: PRIVATE. Postgres-related.
3. app — nested app reference. Schema is currently empty (no properties).

All low priority. Cannot be tested via golden fixtures — these resource types are unstable/preview and cannot be deployed on free edition. Implement when these resource types stabilize or show up in real plans. Test via unit tests with mock plan data.

title: Refactor _call_stack to closure-captured state in debug.py
description: Module-level mutable _call_stack: list[float] = [] in debug.py:10 violates 'no module-level mutable state' guideline. Mutated at runtime via .append()/.pop() in _profile_hook. Refactor to closure-captured state inside enable_profile_tracing() so the mutable list only exists when tracing is enabled.

title: Fine-tune zoom level, initial zoom, and centering
Replace string-derived 'changed' property with explicit boolean field
description: _ActionConfig.changed in text.py:43-44 derives semantics from display string (self.display \!= 'unchanged'). This couples presentation to logic — if someone renames the display label, behavior silently changes. Add an explicit 'changed: bool = True' field to _ActionConfig and set changed=False on the 'skip' entry instead.

title: Terminal width-aware line wrapping for CLI output
description:
Use `os.get_terminal_size().columns` to detect terminal width and wrap long lines with proper continuation indentation instead of letting the terminal wrap at arbitrary points.

**Approach:** Build lines inline as we do now. If a line exceeds the terminal width, wrap it at semantic boundaries (commas between list/dict items, `->` separator) with continuation indented to the field's indent level + offset. Falls back to 80 columns when piped (no tty).

**Lower bound:** Set a minimum width (e.g. 60-80 chars) below which we don't attempt smart wrapping — if your terminal is that narrow, you just live with the wrapping. Above that threshold, lines are wrapped to fit.

**Applies to:** All CLI output lines — field changes (transitions, single values), resource headers. Not just transitions. The multiline block rendering for large single values stays as-is (it's already formatted for readability).

**Open questions:**
- Exact lower bound threshold
- Dumb wrap (textwrap on whitespace) vs structure-aware wrap (break at commas, `->`)
- Whether markdown output should also respect a configurable width or stay unwrapped (markdown renderers handle their own wrapping)

title: CLI --filter flag with search DSL from browser UI
description:
Port the browser search DSL to the CLI as a --filter / -f flag for text output.

The browser already has a search DSL (js/src/utils/search-query-parser.ts) with four token kinds:
- type:X — match resource type badge (jobs, volumes, schemas, etc.)
- status:X — match diff state (added, modified, removed, unchanged)
- "exact" — exact name match
- bare words — fuzzy text match against resource name

All tokens AND together. Same semantics on the CLI.

Examples:
  dagshund plan.json -f 'type:jobs'
  dagshund plan.json -f 'status:added'
  dagshund plan.json -f 'type:volumes status:removed'
  dagshund plan.json -f '"etl_pipeline"'

Composes with --changes-only (dagshund-37yu): filter first, then suppress unchanged within the filtered set.

Implementation notes:
- Python reimplements the tokenizer/matcher (no shared code with JS, but same grammar)
- Matching targets: resource type from the key prefix, action mapped to diff state, resource name
- Empty filter result = no output (not an error)
- No effect on browser mode (-o)

Design consideration: When implementing, consider refactoring _print_resource_groups to accept a predicate function (Callable[[ResourceChange], bool]) instead of the current frozenset[DiffState]. This would let the DSL filter and the diff state filter compose naturally as combined predicates, and avoids coupling the rendering layer to specific filter types.

title: Rename non-verb-first functions to match naming convention
description:
## Problem

Three private functions in `src/dagshund/text.py` use noun/adjective names instead of the required verb-first naming convention. The project guidelines require: "Descriptive names. Verb-first (extractJobTaskEdges not getEdges). No abbreviations."

## Functions to Rename

### 1. `_action_style` → `_resolve_action_style`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 54
- Returns `(color, symbol)` tuple for an action string
- "action_style" is a noun phrase; "resolve" makes it verb-first
- Used in: `_render_resource`, `_print_summary`

### 2. `_display_action` → `_map_display_action`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 59
- Maps internal action names to user-facing display names
- "display" is ambiguous (noun or verb); "map" clarifies intent
- Used in: `_render_resource`

### 3. `_all_unchanged` → `_check_all_unchanged`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 192
- Returns bool indicating whether every resource is unchanged
- "all_unchanged" is an adjective; "check" makes it verb-first
- Alternative: `_is_all_unchanged` (Python predicate convention)
- Used in: `render_text`

## Scope
- Rename the function definitions
- Update all call sites within `src/dagshund/text.py`
- Update all references in test files (`tests/test_text.py`) — the tests import these private functions directly
- Grep the entire repo to make sure no other references are missed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three functions renamed with verb-first names
- All call sites and test references updated
- `just check` passes
- No stale references to old names anywhere in the repo

title: Fix Lakebase hierarchy: identity fields, resource path parsing, rename, fixture
description:
The Lakebase (formerly 'Lakebase Autoscaling') hierarchy has multiple issues from synthetic fixture data and incorrect field assumptions.

## What's wrong

1. **Identity fields**: Code uses `name` for project/branch identity. Real fields are `project_id` and `branch_id`.
2. **Parent references**: Code treats `parent` as a bare name. Real format is resource paths:
   - Branch `parent`: `projects/{project_id}` → points to project
   - Endpoint `parent`: `projects/{project_id}/branches/{branch_id}` → points to branch
3. **Root label**: 'Lakebase Autoscaling' should be just 'Lakebase'
4. **Fixture data**: `all-hierarchies-plan.json` uses wrong field names (`name` instead of `project_id`/`branch_id`/`endpoint_id`, bare names instead of resource paths for `parent`)

## What needs to change

### Code (build-resource-graph.ts)
- POSTGRES_CHAIN tier 0 (project): `resolveIdentity` should extract `project_id` not `name`
- POSTGRES_CHAIN tier 1 (branch): `resolveIdentity` should extract `branch_id` not `name`; `resolveParentRef` should parse `projects/{project_id}` to extract project_id
- POSTGRES_CHAIN tier 2 (endpoint): `resolveParentRef` should parse `projects/{project_id}/branches/{branch_id}` to extract branch_id
- Rename `rootLabel` from 'Lakebase Autoscaling' to 'Lakebase'

### Fixture (all-hierarchies-plan.json)
- Update postgres_projects to use `project_id`, `display_name`, `pg_version` (not `name`, `comment`)
- Update postgres_branches to use `branch_id`, `parent: projects/{project_id}`, `is_protected`, `no_expiry` (not `name`, `parent: bare_name`)
- Update postgres_endpoints to use `endpoint_id`, `parent: projects/{project_id}/branches/{branch_id}`, `endpoint_type` (not `name`, `parent: bare_name`, `type`)
- Add realistic field values matching the docs example

### Real field reference (from docs)

**Project fields**: project_id, display_name, pg_version, history_retention_duration, default_endpoint_settings
**Branch fields**: branch_id, parent (projects/{project_id}), source_branch (projects/{project_id}/branches/{branch_id}), is_protected, no_expiry, expire_time, ttl, source_branch_lsn, source_branch_time
**Endpoint fields**: endpoint_id, parent (projects/{project_id}/branches/{branch_id}), endpoint_type (ENDPOINT_TYPE_READ_WRITE|ENDPOINT_TYPE_READ_ONLY), autoscaling_limit_min_cu, autoscaling_limit_max_cu, disabled, no_suspension, suspend_timeout_duration, settings

### Tests
- Update test cases in build-resource-graph.test.ts to use correct field names and resource path formats

### Optional (separate bead)
- Model `source_branch` as a non-hierarchy provenance edge between branches
close_reason: Identity fields, resource path parsing, root rename, and fixture all updated to match real Databricks API

title: Show lateral dependencies in affected resource detail panels
description: In the browser visualization, when a user opens the detail panel for an affected resource, show its lateral dependencies (other resources it depends on or that depend on it), not just the parent/child DAG edges.

title: Add debug logging and verbose output
description:
Two distinct layers of additional output:

1. **Debug logging** (stdlib logging module, --debug / -d flag or DAGSHUND_DEBUG=1)
   - Traces the full pipeline: input source (file/stdin), JSON parse result, plan extraction, resource grouping, rendering path (text vs browser)
   - Uses Python logging at DEBUG level — conventional, composable with other tools
   - Outputs to stderr so it doesn't interfere with stdout rendering

2. **Verbose mode** (--verbose / -v flag)
   - Extra user-facing information: resource counts per type, field change counts, timing, plan metadata details
   - Not developer diagnostics — useful operational detail for the user

Both should be independent flags that can be combined. Debug implies verbose.

Scope: full pipeline coverage across cli.py, __init__.py, text.py, browser.py.
close_reason: Already implemented in a previous session

title: perf: fix O(n²) algorithms in layout-graph.ts
description: Two algorithmic inefficiencies: (1) topologicalSortTasks (lines 99-117) uses Array.shift() which is O(n) per call, making the overall BFS O(n²) instead of O(n+E). Fix: use index-based queue (maintain front pointer). (2) buildElkCompoundGraph (lines 170-180) filters edges with O(T*E) linear scans via .some(). Fix: build a Set of task IDs per group and use .has() for O(1) lookup. Both are theoretical concerns for typical Databricks DAG sizes but easy fixes.

title: fix: edge colors don't update on theme toggle
description: getEdgeStyle in diff-state-styles.ts calls getComputedStyle to resolve CSS variables at layout time, baking in the values. When the user toggles theme, edges retain old colors while nodes update. Fix: use var(--edge-added) etc as inline style values instead of resolved colors, so the browser resolves them reactively.

description: src/cli.ts:216 uses template literal ${tmpDir}/dagshund-${randomUUID()}.html without stripping trailing slash from XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, producing /run/user/1000//dagshund-.... Fix with path.join() or trimming the trailing slash.

title: Review golden fixtures for clarity and coverage
description:
Walk through each golden fixture directory and evaluate: (1) Is the fixture name clear and descriptive? (2) Does it test a distinct, meaningful scenario? (3) Are there redundant or missing cases? (4) Do the fixture contents (YAML, expected output) match what the name promises? Human-driven review — go fixture by fixture together.

Fixtures need to be extended to cover:
- Dashboard resources with warehouse_id lateral edges
- Dashboard tasks in jobs referencing both dashboard_id and warehouse_id
- Other job task types (sql_task, dbt_task, notebook_task, alert_task, power_bi_task) referencing warehouse_id
- Quality monitors referencing warehouse_id
- Job pipeline_task referencing a DLT pipeline via pipeline_id (dagshund-247c)
- Serving endpoint referencing a registered_model that is absent from the plan (dagshund-1o0m)
- App with uc_securable reference — already present in app-dependencies fixture but ignored by code (dagshund-33dm)
- Quality monitor with table_name three-part UC reference (dagshund-6334)

---

## Session log

### Fixture #1: all-create — DONE (2026-04-14)

Reshape (landed earlier on main):
- Dropped dangling experiments.audit_analysis + registered_models.quality_metrics
- Dropped unused analytics.raw_data volume
- Renamed external_imports → landing (ETL landing zone in analytics_staging)
- Added trigger_quality_checks task to etl_pipeline with run_job_task → data_quality_pipeline
- Updated narrative header

Discovery: run_job_task had no lateral edge. Spun off as dagshund-aaby, landed in commit a86fd85.

Final review: considered adding a quality_monitor on analytics.curated_events to tighten narrative + cover phantom-table lateral edge, but the table wouldn't exist yet on a first deploy — QM creation would fail at apply time. Coverage for 3-part table_name QMs is already in lateral-deps (dagshund-6334, closed). Decision: leave all-create as-is, minor narrative snags accepted.

### Fixture #2: all-destroy — DONE

Comment cleanup only — no structural changes. Fixture correctly exercises every Dangerous Actions warning path (experiments, registered_models, schemas, volumes) via a broad resource set.
- before/ comment: dropped stale "Same resource set as all-create/after" claim, added decommissioning narrative + explanation of why it's intentionally broader
- after/ comment: added note that it pairs with before/ to exercise dangerous-action warnings

### Fixture #3: all-hierarchies — DONE (kept, de-emphasized)

Confirmed synthetic — hand-authored plan.json with no before/after YAML pair, exotic resource types (catalogs, database_*, postgres_*, synced_database_tables, external_locations) not reproducible in OSS workspace, cli_version drift (0.290.0 vs 0.296.0). Removes options for `just regen`.
- Kept fixture (Option C) — deleting it would break ~10 tests across 3 files that cover exotic resource type code paths
- Removed Pages index card
- Added README.md explaining synthetic nature, listing types it uniquely covers, warning against regen
- Filed dagshund-198b to replace with a real enterprise-workspace bundle

### Fixture #4: app-dependencies — DONE

Reshaped from identical before/after (no-op plan) to a **second deploy** story: the ETL pipeline has matured enough to plug in experiment tracking, a secret-backed external API call, and LLM inference.

before/: unchanged baseline — app with 3 refs (job, sql_warehouse, uc_securable), dangling experiment, jobs/my_etl_job.

after/: adds new model_serving_endpoints/app_chat_endpoint (bundle-owned, served from phantom_schema.phantom_model) and wires 3 new refs into the app:
- experiment_tracker (de-dangles my_experiment)
- api_token (phantom secret → CLI-managed `app_secrets` scope)
- chat_model (the new serving endpoint)

Plan shows: `+ model_serving_endpoints/app_chat_endpoint (create)`, `~ apps/my_test_app (update)`, `= experiments/my_experiment`, `= jobs/my_etl_job`.

#### Bugs surfaced and fixed inline

1. **experiment_id vs id mismatch** in `extractAppResourceReferences`: extractor read `experiment["id"]` but DAB emits `experiment["experiment_id"]` (per schema). Check never matched → no app→experiment lateral edge. Fix: read `experiment["experiment_id"]`. Updated 2 tests in extract-lateral-edges.test.ts that had stale fixture payloads.

2. **Phantom type badges missing in Dependencies panel**: `connected-nodes.ts` `toEntry` used `extractTypeBadge` for the "Depends on" / "Depended on by" lists, which only handles real resource keys (`resources.type.name`). Phantom IDs like `sql-warehouse::…`, `secret-scope::…`, `source-table::…` fell through. Fix: use `extractPhantomBadge` which handles both phantoms and falls through to extractTypeBadge for real resources. Warehouse/secret/table/experiment phantoms now show their (type) badge in the panel.

#### Filed during review

- dagshund-an5c (P2 feature, discovered-from vi8p) — Element-wise diff for list[object] fields in text + markdown renderers. The app.resources[] diff rendered as whole-array dumps (2 massive inline-JSON lines). Exhaustive description covering affected fields (apps.resources, jobs.tasks, depends_on, libraries, served_entities, permissions), three rendering approaches, recommended hybrid, regression plan, new test cases, out-of-scope notes.

#### Not addressed (minor)

- bundle.name `dagshund-golden-app-deps` vs dir `app-dependencies` — naming drift vs all other fixtures, cosmetic, left as-is
- Empty `app_src/app.py` — DAB doesn't care, skipped the "placeholder comment" touch

### Remaining fixtures (top-to-bottom)

- [ ] lateral-deps
- [ ] manual-drift
- [ ] mixed-changes
- [ ] no-changes
- [ ] sub-resources
- [ ] task-dag-rewiring

title: Revisit resource type badge design (length, positioning, styling)
description: The full Databricks resource type names (e.g. 'synced database table', 'postgres endpoint', 'database instance') are quite long as badges on nodes. Need to reconsider: truncation strategy, abbreviation rules, positioning/layout of badges, font size, whether to use the full resource type name or a shorter form. Should feel consistent across all resource types.

title: Unify selectedNode and selectedNodeId into single useState
description:
flow-canvas.tsx:45-46 — selectedNode (DagNodeData|null) and selectedNodeId (string|null) are always set/cleared in lockstep across 3 call sites (handleClosePanel, handlePaneClick, handleNodeClick). Could unify into useState<{ data: DagNodeData; id: string } | null> to eliminate structural drift risk.

Trade-off: saves one useState + one setter per mutation site, but adds destructuring at ~4 consumption sites. The two effectiveSelected* useMemo derivations would still be needed for the phantom-hidden check. Net value is marginal — the 3 mutation sites are all in the same file, so drift risk is low in practice.

Won't-fix for now. Revisit if flow-canvas state grows further or if selection logic moves to a hook.

title: fix: sanitize input file path in CLI
description: readPlanFromFile takes path from args without sanitization. Theoretical path traversal if CLI invoked programmatically with untrusted input. Fix: resolve to absolute path, optionally validate .json extension, warn on '..' sequences.

title: recreate action should count as both add and delete in summary
description: The Databricks CLI counts 'recreate' in both 'to add' AND 'to delete' summary buckets. We currently count it as 'modified' only. Since recreate is destructive (deletes and recreates the resource), it should be flagged more prominently — at minimum counted correctly in the summary, possibly with a warning similar to stateful resource delete warnings.
close_reason: won't fix — our ~N recreate summary is more accurate than the CLI's double-counting; stateful resource warnings already flag the destructive nature

title: Browser UI: handle all-skip plans and display 'unchanged' instead of 'skip'
description:
The browser visualization shows 'no plan' when given a plan where all resources have action 'skip' (e.g. fixtures/no-changes-plan.json). The JS pipeline likely filters out skip resources somewhere, leaving an empty graph.

## What needs investigating
1. Where in the JS parsing/graph pipeline are skip resources filtered out?
2. Does the app show 'no plan' because the graph ends up empty after filtering?

## What needs fixing
- All-skip plans should render meaningfully (e.g. show the DAG with all nodes styled as 'unchanged')
- The mapping layer (map-diff-state.ts) already maps skip → unchanged correctly
- The issue is likely in graph building or task extraction filtering out skip resources entirely

## Context
- Python text mode fix landed in dagshund-3ebj: skip → 'unchanged' labels, all-skip shows 'No changes (N resources unchanged)'
- Browser already has correct DiffState styling for 'unchanged' nodes
- Fixture: fixtures/no-changes-plan.json (8 resources, all action: skip)
close_reason: Fixed: resolveTaskEntries/resolveJobState fall back to remote_state when new_state is absent

title: visual: restyle React Flow controls (+/−/center/lock) and minimap to match theme
description: The native React Flow Controls component (+, −, fit view, lock) and MiniMap look out of place — they use React Flow's default styling which doesn't match the dagshund theme. Restyle or replace with custom components that match our design tokens.

title: Remove redundant sorted() on already-sorted dict keys
description: text.py:193 calls sorted(resource_groups) but the dict keys are already in sorted order from _group_by_resource_type which sorts before groupby. Python 3.7+ dicts preserve insertion order. The sorted() is redundant. However it does make the ordering guarantee visible locally — consider adding a comment instead of removing it.

title: fix: increase light mode --diff-added-soft alpha for better contrast
description: R3-MED-20. In light mode, --diff-added-soft: rgba(88,117,57,0.16) barely registers against --surface: #c8cad3. Added nodes don't stand out enough compared to the vivid red for removed. Fix: increase alpha on --diff-added-soft to at least 0.24 in light mode. Located in js/src/styles/index.css around lines 21-22. Compare against WCAG AA contrast requirements. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Property-based tests for merge_sub_resources with Hypothesis
description:
src/dagshund/merge.py is the gnarliest logic in the repo (sub-resource absorption into parents, action promotion, state injection, self-referential dep filtering). Today it's covered by example tests + golden end-to-end fixtures. Property tests would prove invariants instead of just spot-checking them.

Add hypothesis to the dev dependency group. Invariants worth proving:

1. Orphan preservation — if a sub-resource key has no matching parent in the plan, merge_sub_resources preserves it verbatim under the original sub-resource key. (Already a code path in merge.py:180, worth a property not just an example.)

2. Idempotence on sub-free input — if the input has no sub-resource keys (no keys with >3 dot segments), merge_sub_resources(x) == x.

3. Action promotion rules in _promote_action:
   - parent='skip' or '', sub='update' => parent becomes 'update'
   - parent='delete', sub='update' => stays 'delete' (destructive wins)
   - parent='create', sub='create' => stays 'create'
   Generate action pairs, assert the promotion table.

4. depends_on sanity:
   - Self-referential edges (node == parent_key) never appear in merged output
   - Sub-resource-key targets are rewritten to their parent key

5. Field-key prefixing in _prefix_changes: for any sub with suffix S and field key K, the merged parent has a change at '{S}.{K}'. If the parent already has a field named '{S}.{K}' verbatim, document resolution order (current code: prefixed sub wins via dict spread order — is that intended?).

Each invariant is ~5 lines of @given. Not urgent — the code is stable and golden fixtures exercise it end-to-end. File under 'do when in a refactoring mood' or 'do alongside dagshund-161c to catch regressions during the typed-model refactor'.

Luxury, not priority. Skip if bored of the repo.

title: Full Python codebase review and address findings
description: Review all Python code (src/dagshund/, tests/) for code quality, pattern consistency, and CLAUDE.md/PYTHON.md compliance. Address findings in the same bead — review, present to human, fix what's approved, file beads for the rest.

title: refactor: replace sys.exit() in library functions with DagshundError exceptions
description: R3-HIGH-2. Multiple Python functions that are NOT the entry point call sys.exit(1) directly: browser.py (_find_template, _validate_plan_json), text.py (render_text JSON validation), cli.py (read_plan). This makes the code untestable without subprocess isolation and impossible to use as a library. Fix: create a DagshundError exception class. Have library functions raise it instead of calling sys.exit. Let main() in cli.py be the only place that catches DagshundError and calls sys.exit with the message. This also consolidates the duplicated JSON validation from browser.py and text.py into a shared _parse_plan(raw) function. Flagged by: Architect (MEDIUM), Code Quality (MEDIUM x3).

title: Investigate 3x filtering in render_text pipeline
description:
render_text() passes visible_states and resource_filter to three functions independently: _print_resource_groups, _print_summary, and _collect_warnings. Each re-filters the same resource set.

This is the same pattern that existed before the --filter feature — not a regression. And N is small (typical plans have 10-50 resources), so there's no measurable performance issue today.

However, it's worth investigating whether a single-pass design is feasible. The challenge: _print_resource_groups needs both the total count and filtered count per group for headers like 'jobs (2/5)'. Pre-filtering at the top of render_text would lose the total.

Possible approaches to explore:
- Filter once, pass both original and filtered dicts downstream
- Have _filter_resources return (filtered, total_count) tuples
- Restructure render_text to group and filter in one pass, then distribute results

Investigation only — don't fix unless the design is clearly simpler than what exists.

title: Hover task → highlight node and dependency edges
description:
When a user hovers over a task node, visually highlight that task and its immediate dependency edges.

### Behavior
- Mouse enters task node → highlight the node (brighter border or glow) and all edges directly connected to it (both incoming and outgoing)
- Non-connected edges and nodes should dim slightly to create contrast
- Mouse leaves → restore normal styling
- Should feel responsive (no animation delay on enter, subtle fade on leave is OK)

### Implementation approach
- Use React Flow's `onNodeMouseEnter` / `onNodeMouseLeave` callbacks
- Track hovered node ID in state
- Apply conditional CSS classes to edges based on whether they connect to the hovered node
- Could use React Flow's `useEdges` or custom edge styling

### Files to modify
- `src/App.tsx` or `src/hooks/` — hover state management
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` — hover visual feedback on the node itself
- Edge styling in App.tsx or a custom edge component

### Styling
Highlighted edges: brighter color (white or accent). Dimmed elements: reduced opacity. Keep it subtle — this is a wayfinding aid, not a light show.

title: Code clarity: add comments for magic numbers, edge priority, and clean up noise
description:
Five clarity findings from TS review:

1. **Magic 0.5 in structural-diff-view.tsx** (line 41): Line-wrap algorithm scans backward up to 50% of line width for a break point. Add a named constant or comment.

2. **MAX_LINE_CHARS comment mismatch** (structural-diff-view.tsx:19): Comment says 'fits ~300px' but panel is resizable 300-600px. Update comment to note this is a conservative lower bound.

3. **Edge styling priority undocumented** (flow-canvas.tsx:198-238): Three visual states (hover > filter > selection) interact with a priority chain. Add a brief comment at the top of the useMemo explaining the precedence.

4. **Redundant console.error in browser hooks** (use-plan-graph.ts:27, use-resource-graph.ts:22): Layout errors are already surfaced via error state in the UI. The console.error leaks into the exported HTML's browser console. Remove them.

5. **Test description imprecision** (html-assembler.test.ts): Says 'case-insensitively' but the function normalizes to lowercase. Fix the test description.
